bs-(between-stabbings)/bs-rolls-(checks).title: BS ROLLS (CHECKS)
bs-(between-stabbings)/bs-rolls-(checks).description:
Used to resolve arguments or environmental obstacles. Pool: 2d6 Base + Prism X d6. Success: Each die showing 4, 5, or 6. Sixes count as double.
Failure: Each die showing 3, 2, or 1.
Difficulty: DC 1-2: Standard Task. DC 3: Strenuous. DC 4: Dangerous/Elite. DC 6+: Near-impossible. Extra Difficulty: Roll 2x of the same Value (ex. 2x3) - Precise Task
Roll 3x of the same Value - Surgical Task
Roll 4x of the same Value - Stars must Align...

bs-(between-stabbings)/level_up.html.title: LEVEL_UP
bs-(between-stabbings)/level_up.html.description:
Level-Ups occur on NODE - REST/between EXPLORATIONs, and use SHARDS accumulated during travels, which allow for: Increasing a single Prism's Value (-5 shards)
Removing Cards (-3 shards) *A player’s deck must always contain at least 4 BACKBONE Cards from their Core selection.
Adding Keywords to Cards (-3 shards for Primary, -2 shards for Secondary and Tertiary, -1 for CURSED) When adding a Keyword to a card, you may (only) choose a Keyword that scales with a Prism you have at least 1 point in.
Purging permanent STATUS cards (-3 shards)
Adding new BACKBONE Cards (-2 shards) *When adding a new Card, it is encouraged to follow the New Keyword rule from above, unless you are planning several steps ahead of course. Changing Keywords already on Cards (-2)
Restoring EXHAUSTed cards (-1) Removing Keywords from Cards (+1)

bs-(between-stabbings)/shards.html.title: SHARDS
bs-(between-stabbings)/shards.html.description:
Shards are the universal currency of the Hearth. They act as both long-term progression and can be crushed as mid-combat consumables: Cost: 0 ENERGY.
Effect: grant +1 ENERGY AND +1 DRAW Crushed Shards are gone forever and cannot be used for progression. MIGHT = enemies defeated. 1 enemy kill = 1 Shard/10 HP the enemy had. INSIGHT = full intent loops realized (understanding the enemy). 1 FULL intent Loop realised = 3 Shard. GUILE = fewer turns taken / efficiency. Baseline Turns (=2x Enemy count - N (Player count)). Baseline Turns - Turns Taken = 2 Shards each. Thus the rewards push a “pick 2 of 3” shape: get out of there fast, watch and learn, You can’t get both every fight.
There is a cap of 2xN SHARDS that are "active" in the shared deck at any time, to prevent deck pollution and overspending what is essentially XP points.

character/oaths/conduit/oath_of_the_cond.title: OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT
character/oaths/conduit/oath_of_the_cond.description:
Your Prism scores are as follows: 0 MIGHT
1 GUILE
2 INSIGHT You may allocate a single point to a chosen Prism. Your Starting deck consists of:
3x STRIKE
3x GUARD
2x EMPOWER
2x MEND

character/oaths/mistwalker/oath_of_the_m.title: OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER
character/oaths/mistwalker/oath_of_the_m.description:
Your Prism scores are as follows: 1 MIGHT
2 GUILE
0 INSIGHT You may allocate a single point to a chosen Prism. Your Starting deck consists of:
3x STRIKE
5x GUARD
2x COUNTER

ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/rulings.ht.title: RULINGS
ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/rulings.ht.description:
Enemies operate on fixed values and unique behaviours, ignoring player Prism constraints. This allows the GM to create "impossible" threats that require specific card(s) or behaviour as answers. An enemy's "Statblock" is essentially their Intent Loop/Algorithm + their HP. Ex.: Husk = 7 HP Intent: Strike (6) -> Guard (8) -> Strike (8) -> Guard (12) -> LOOP. This makes enemies predictable (rewards knowledge/experience and learning), and decreases GM's mental load drastically, while keeping the players on their toes whenever they meet something new. Enemy GUARD does* stack innately. Enemies are tracked by laying out their individual cards in a 5-wide and 3-tall grid.*
The Frontline is composed of the first two rows, while the third row is reserved for REINFORCEMENTS. Some enemy Loops may change depending on their position. Potential enemy-tracker card. Has space for Name, two diamonds for HP and Guard, big box for an illustration, and a semicircle meant to house their intent.*

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/energy..title: ENERGY
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/energy..description:
Used to play cards. The cost of a card equals the number of Keywords on it. A Party's ENERGY Max is calculated as follows: N (no. Players) X 2. The Hearth replenishes all its ENERGY at every turn start unless noted otherwise.
The Maximum ENERGY available can also be influenced by the SYNC-AXIS.

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/sync-ax.title: SYNC-AXIS
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/sync-ax.description:
The relationship between the Embers dictates their combat performance and playstyle. It should mainly be the Players that decide when the marker moves across the Axis, as they roleplay among themselves. The scale ranges from -3 to +3, and is tied to ENERGY and DMG variables. GM rule of thumb:
Always judge the SYNC-AXIS by the weakest chain/bond.

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/turns.h.title: TURNS
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/turns.h.description:
Fights are a constant back-and-forth between the Hearth-linked Embers and their opponents, following a simple flow: Setup. - Start-of-encounter effects. - Player passives and Artefacts trigger. - Enemy passives can trigger here. - Initial intents are revealed. Player turn. - Cards are dealt (N x 2). - Energy is regained (N x 2). - Players play and resolve their cards in order. - The rest are discarded, if possible.
- Excess energy gives +2 DRAW the next round. Enemy turn. - Intents are realized left to right, starting at the second line. Then left to right on the first line. End of round. - Bleed/Ignite tick.
- Player GUARDis removed - Reinforcements arrive. - New intents are shown. After round 1, the game becomes: Player turn → Enemy turn → End of round Cleaning the Deck.
Remove any non-Trauma (see VITALS) STATUS cards (with the exception of HOLLOW). This includes UNPLAYABLE cards with RETAIN from the players' hands. Do not reorder or shuffle the remaining cards in the process. Clearing the Hearth Sheet.
Remove any Negative Stacks such as BLEED, or IGNITE.
Positive Stacks, such as MOMENTUM, BRACE, or COUNTER are Halved, then kept for the next encounter.
Receive SHARDS as per their formula
Only up to 2xN SHARDS can be inside the deck at once. The rest are simply placed aside for the next LEVEL_UP or,
If any SHARDS were spent during combat, replenish them up to their cap.

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/vitals..title: VITALS
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/vitals..description:
This is the Sum of All Players' Prisms + N (no. Players) X 2 When Vitals hits 0, the deck's top card is flipped, and its owner must decide: Shatter: Immediately remove 1 of their CUSTOMS of choice from the deck. Culling: The player reduces one Prism score by 1 (Post-combat).
Trauma: Add a random, persistent STATUS card from this selection:
WOUND
PANIC
DOUBT
INJURY
TREMBLING
These cards stay in the deck unless specifically removed using SHARDS. The owner is required to take the card they "caused" whenever it appears during play, ignoring the standard STATUS card dealing rules.
Regardless of their decision, N x STATUS cards are shuffled into the shared deck. These cards are based on the Party's Oath choices:
OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER causes a MIST
OATH_OF_THE_PYRE cases an EROSION
OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT causes a HOLLOW
Each subsequent hit also shuffles in a stacking amount of ASH cards (for a 3-player group this would look like so);
1st Hit: 1x MIST, 1x EROSION, 1x HOLLOW.
2nd Hit: 1x MIST, 1x EROSION, 1x HOLLOW + 1x ASH.
3rd Hit: 1x MIST, 1x EROSION, 1x HOLLOW. + 2x ASH.
If the topdeck revealed card is a STATUS one itself, the player with the most CHARGE, or Highest Prism score sum is the decisionmaker. Max Vitals are recalculated at combat end only.
When hit at 0 Vitals, it is also an option to Sacrifice oneself. The player's character dies, but, they release their CHARGE immediately, with no extra costs nor limitations. Their Cards are then taken ot of the deck, and replaced with HOLLOW.
Death can also occur when an Ember's Prism scores all hit 0. The Ember's cards are then still removed from the deck, but are not replaced with any STATUS cards.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/cin.title: CINDERS
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/cin.description: Just as there are the Hearth-linked Embers, there are also "stray Embers", or "Cinders". Embers without a Hearth, either because of tragedy, or neglect, they suffer alone. These individuals make for great adversaries. Ranging from "Mad geniuses" who "made it work", to individuals broken by trauma, lashing out at the unjust world.They consume others. A Cinder doesn't have their Hearth; they seek Fuel. They kidnap people and "burn" through them to stabilize themselves. This gives the players a clear, gritty objective: stop the Cinder from using the local village as a life support. Cinder Status: If Cinders "lose their grip on reality," perhaps they are actually still "perceiving" the bond. They might be trying to talk to the ghosts of their former Hearth-mates, or their brains are permanently wired to receive signals that are no longer being sent.Power isn’t skill. It’s lack of restraint mechanisms. No bond damping No long-term planning They burn hot, And they know it. Which makes them terrifying.

headers.heading: If Hearth-bonding is so necessary, how do these solo adversaries survive?

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/crs.title: CRS
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/crs.description: Government-dependent bodies that handle the population's EROSION. They set up facilities of cold stone and water to allow for safe venting, as well as taking in the "terminal" cases, which they handle out back. They wear layered uniforms, like toxic hazard workers. High-status, low-life-expectancy, trained to read early collapse signs. People accept: Lantern taxes Data indexing Witchhunt-like inspections Not because they trust authority, but because they’ve seen what happens when someone collapses in a crowd.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/hea.title: HEARTHS AND EMBERS
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/hea.description:
People with spiked Prism aptitudes. The cost of power is life. Embers suffer a shortened lifespan, burning their years away the moment this flame blooms within them for the first time. Despite that, it's a gift, one that manifests randomly and powerfully, and comes at a horrid price. Players are called Embers, groups of bonded Embers are called Hearths. This bond acts as a "Resonance Buffer," preventing the individuals from "detonating" or collapsing into a Hollow state. Hearths can form due to: Rituals done by the Embers themselves, or even without the Embers' choice/knowledge via grimoires or other artifacts. Life experiences: a Bond is likely to form during a shared traumatic event, or, for the lucky ones, it can build up after years of closeness with one another. Bonding with other Embers allows for cleaner channelling - which translates nicely into the mechanics of extra energy per party member. Because Embers forget their pasts (due to LORE/WORLD/CULTURAL PILLAR/RELIGION-PHILOSOPHY/EROSION), they often lack a traditional moral compass. They act on instinct, immediate survival, or the orders of whoever is currently guiding them.
Hearth-members (Embers) are known to have blurry memories and little recollection of who they were before the "merge". The bond between them can also be strengthened or weakened by individual interactions, even to the point of breaking (which is a traumatic experience on its own) It is rare to survive a bond breaking, but those who do are called CINDERS, and often lose their grip on reality, turning away from society altogether Everyone in a hearth is a bit of an anchor to everyone else. Usually the other members are enough to "ground" whoever's having a tough time, but in times of high stress the emotions may get amplified/cascadeHearths: Distributed Containment A Hearth is a living heat-sharing network. Soul-binding isn’t mystical romance. It’s engineering under duress; Heat disperses across multiple vessels Panic dampens if others are stable Emotional regulation is forced by proximity Individual spikes become manageable averages 3–5 was not chosen by accident: 2 is unstable (feedback loop) 6+ increases coordination failure 3–5 balances redundancy and control Someone absolutely did the math. On corpses.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/the.title: THE EARTHBOUND
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/the.description: These zealots believe in extremist pacifism, claiming that every life spared and death delayed brings HELL closer to catching up, and ridding the world of magic and GREY MIST. They believe that emotions, memories, and identity are the "fuel" that keeps the furnace overtaxed. Their faith is a rigorous practice of ego-death and emotional suppression. By becoming cold inside while alive, they hope to pass through Hell like a ghost through a flame—unprocessed and unnoticed. They take vows of silence and use lead-lined masks to "keep their heat in."

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/the.title: THE GREAT HEARTH
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/the.description: A movement striving to unite all of humanity in one giant Hearth, they believe that will stabilise everyone and bring about the harmony observed among the more stable Hearths. Their methods are faulty, unorthodox, and oftentimes forceful, which turned the public opinion against them after some disastrous attempts at joining 20 people on a city square that no longer exists...Individual identity is the problem. They talk about equilibrium and "global balance". Forced soul-binding rituals Artifact-driven mass merges City-scale bonding attempts Kidnapping Embers first (they’re “keystones”) Because every failure: Produces survivors who swear it almost worked. Creates permanent hot zones they interpret as “holy ground”. Generates Cinders they call Heralds.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/war.title: WAR
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/war.description: Is what the peoples of this world call the event that first ignited the spiral of suffering the world's been cast into ever since continues still, but fractured and localised as independent cities and states lunged at the resources near them with little regard for their previous neighbours.New maps are drawn on the regular to picture the supposed control each region holds, however nobody but the Hollows truly rules within the GREY MIST.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/wet.title: WET RATS
lore/world/cultural-pillar/fractions/wet.description: Black Market Releases, the best alternative to the CRS Because of course. Illegal venting in slums. Portable release charms. Collapse-for-hire sabotage. Most Hollow outbreaks trace back here. But the alternative, for the unfortunate, is so much worse.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/religion-phil.title: EROSION
lore/world/cultural-pillar/religion-phil.description:
Magic doesn’t want to be inside you. Veins, fingernails, and even eyes may begin glowing in red/yellow hues. That's the usual sign of magic accumulation, which, if left untreated, can cause "burnups" "Treating it" means releasing the magic outward, which itself has a chance for burning the person, but also their surroundings if uncontrolled) Usually the Hearth's bond is enough to allow for venting as well as controlled releases of build up magic, but ice, water, and other means of staying cool are what the general public has to work with. The "end product" of the magic accumulation's prolonged effect on a human body is being turned into a Husk - zombie-like shell of a being that resembles a burn victim at least, and becomes vaguely human at worst. A Husk isn’t undead in the usual sense. Collapse = total soul overload. What remains is: A body whose tissues have been thermally vitrified from the inside out. A mind erased by the intensity of the experience. A shell still moving because the residual heat hasn’t dissipated yet. Moral Baseline Shift When everyone knows: Collapse is possible Husks exist And they were people yesterday The moral bar drops. “I’m a liar. I’m a cheat. But I haven’t collapsed.” That’s enough. Bravery gets you: Overexertion Battlefield surges Collapse Cowards: Flee early Hoard less power Survive. There is no redemption in collapse. No lesson. No chosen fate. A Hollow isn’t punished, sinful, or unworthy. They’re what happens when the system fails.


Just as Dangerous (For Different Reasons) Uncontrolled release disturbs the local atmosphere. Side effects: Fire behaving wrong (screaming flames, fire that resembles faces) Attracting Hollows Creating permanent hot zones

lore/world/cultural-pillar/religion-phil.title: HELL
lore/world/cultural-pillar/religion-phil.description: Hell is not a kingdom of punishment, and it is not a moral scoreboard. It is a processing furnace: a vast metaphysical underworld machine designed to strip souls down, burn off residue, and send what remains into the Hall of Echoes. It was built for throughput, not mercy.Then the Great War happened, and death arrived in quantities the system was never meant to handle. Hell did not break cleanly. It overloaded, backed up, and began to hold what it could not finish processing, decade after decade of spiritual backlog turning the underworld into a clogged pressure chamber. The result is not a dramatic apocalypse but a sustained failure state: a world sitting under an exhausted infernal engine that is still running, still failing, and still trying to catch up.That overflow is the setting’s drive. The pressure leak from Hell becomes the living world’s magic, seeping upward through people, places, and mistakes. Power is therefore not a blessing so much as a symptom: every Ember is living close to the threshold of overload, and every use of magic is a small negotiation with a system that already has too much inside it. This is why fire is easy to start, why control is rare, and why collapse is never far away.Hell also matters as a political and cultural fact, not just a cosmological one. People may not understand the machinery, but they understand the consequences: burnups, Hollowing, the need for venting, the fear of crowds, and the fact that someone who fails to release their pressure safely may become a danger to everyone nearby. That makes the whole world feel like a place built around emergency management, local damage control, and the quiet terror of systems that are always one breakdown away from cascading failure.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/images.html.title: IMAGES

lore/world/physical-pillar/grey-mist.htm.title: GREY MIST
lore/world/physical-pillar/grey-mist.htm.description:
While EROSION comes from the unprocessed, wandering Souls, the Mist is the Will. It doesn't carry emotions, its suffocatingly cold, and imbued with memories, beliefs. Venturing into it is almost guaranteed to cause hallucinations, temporary personality disorders, and psychosis from mind-homogenisation. The leak is global. Gravity, wind, and geography don’t matter much when the source is outside reality. Low light allows it to accumulate. Heat and light disperse it. Cities burn lanterns even at noon. Darkness isn’t romantic, but hazardous.

bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/explo.title: EXPLORATION
bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/explo.description: Exploration is done on a Hex-grid, where the contents of specific Hexes are revealed only a short distance away, but the path as a whole can be viewed instantly (Boardgame reality) It is possible to split the party but doing so usually harbours more risk than reward. To move, every player rolls a D6, then, the rolled sides are added together, and divided by N. Basically: Every time your rolls add up to N, get +1 Movement, and keep adding any rolled dice left. You can then move as far as your Movement allows on the map, and repeat the process.

bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.title: NODE - ENCOUNTER
bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.description: A node hosting a combat or social encounter, marked appropriately depending on the hostility of the situation and the threat level.

bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.title: NODE - PATH
bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.description: An empty hex, allows free travel at a steady pace.

bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.title: NODE - REST
bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.description: A space designated for LEVEL_UP and rejuvenation. Reusable.

bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.title: NODE - UNKNOWN
bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.description:
Its in the name. If no light source or hazmat equipment is in use, Add 1x MADNESS Contains: 1-25 - Enemy Encounter (24%) 26-59 - Empty (35%) 60-69 - A Single Shard (11%)
70-100 - A single STATUS from this selection (1d6): (30%)
WOUND
PANIC
DOUBT
INJURY
TREMBLING
MIST

bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.title: NODE - UNSTABLE
bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.description: A node that creates a one-way path once crossed, cannot be crossed again.

character/oaths/pyre/oath_of_the_pyre.ht.title: OATH_OF_THE_PYRE
character/oaths/pyre/oath_of_the_pyre.ht.description:
Your Prism scores are as follows: 2 MIGHT
1 GUILE
0 INSIGHT You may allocate a single point to a chosen Prism. Your Starting deck consists of: Either;
6x STRIKE
3x GUARD Or;
6x GUARD
3x STRIKE
And, 1x MOMENTUM

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/deck.ht.title: DECK
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/deck.ht.description:
Each turn, players draw cards using the N x 3 formula, from the shared deck, and take their own cards into their Hands. STATUS cards are dealt out evenly as described.
The Hand limit is 5 cards, unless overruled by an Artefact or narrative effects. If you cannot hold any more cards, and they have already been drawn, they are moved to the discard pile immediately and generate no CHARGE charges while doing so.
Once the shared deck runs dry, a Reshuffle is necessary, however, each Reshuffle also adds N status cards, akin to the 0 VITALS situation (also stacking, meaning the third reshuffle will pollute the shared deck with 3xN cards). The Reshuffle counter only resets through a rest, so drawing out one encounter will make you suffer in the next one...
There is a cap of 2xN SHARDS that are "active" in the shared deck at any time, to prevent deck pollution and overspending what is essentially XP points.

character/prisms/guile.html.title: GUILE
character/prisms/guile.html.description: Precision, resource management, and reactive defence. Embers specialising in GUILE think on their feet, under pressure and under fire. They're the ones who pop their heads up first when something occurs, but they will seldom use force, instead relying on their instinct, and their Hearth. They add a sense of competence into the bond, a feeling that any problem can be solved if everyone just thinks for a second. Keywords: GUARD, BRACE, COUNTER

character/prisms/insight.html.title: INSIGHT
character/prisms/insight.html.description: Tactical manipulation, force multiplication, and metaphysical stability. Embers specialising in INSIGHT are usually the strangest of the three, distant and collected, they bring this stability and calm into the Hearth, as they balance the other Oaths' strengths and weaknesses, the only flaw they possess is dependency. Keywords: EMPOWER, IGNITE, MEND.

character/prisms/might.html.title: MIGHT
character/prisms/might.html.description: Physical force, momentum, and brute-force attrition. Embers specialising in MIGHT are usually assertive, problem-oriented, and back down only when struck down. They bring a sense of power and pride into the Hearth, often keeping the morale up by sheer presence alone. Keywords: STRIKE, MOMENTUM, BLEED

character/items/inventory.html.title: INVENTORY
character/items/inventory.html.description:
Inventory is strictly divided to separate narrative utility from mechanical progression. Tools (3 Slots): Functional items TOOLS. These grant Utility,* Advantage (+Success) on specific BS ROLLS (CHECKS), or remove Gatekeeping. Gatekeeping: Certain tasks (climbing a sheer cliff) are impossible (DC ) without the specific item (Rope). 3 Tool slots can be converted into a Curio Slot, but not the other way around.
Curios (1 Slot): Personal narrative items or unique finds. CURIOS can influence one Ember at a time. Enhancements are installed body augments. You may install any number.
Each ENHANCMENTS has an Enhancement Strain tied to it. Your body can support Enhancement Strain up to your PRISM SUM.
At TURN start, if your total Enhancement Strain is above your PRISM SUM, shuffle 1 PSYCHOSIS into your discard pile for each point over the limit.
PSYCHOSIS: When drawn, discard 1 card from your hand, OR lose 1 ENERGY. UNPLAYABLE. EXHAUST at turn end.
This check is made per Ember, so multiple party members can each generate their own PSYCHOSIS. Shards (Numerical Value): Weightless essence stored directly in the Ember Deck. Coin Pouch: Traditional currency used for mundane trade (food, lodging, information).
Armaments (Swords, Guns, Other) are Narrative Justifications. They do not occupy slots because they are the physical justification of your 10-card deck. A player doesn't "get a sword" for +1 DMG; they have STRIKE cards because they have means to deal that DMG (like a pistol, sabre, or dedicated fist).

character/items/artefacts.html.title: ARTEFACTS
character/items/artefacts.html.description:
These are rare, equippable items that sit on the Hearth Sheet (Alongside VITALS, ENERGY, etc.). They provide non-Oath Passive Effects to the Hearth. Examples would Include:
DRAW +1 card whenever you draw a STATUS card.
When playing a STRIKE and a GUARD consecutively add SYNC-AXIS score to both. (Negative values are treated as positive) The Final card played each turn gains +1 to its highest value keyword for every card played before it this turn.
Not playing a GUARD grants +2 DRAW next turn.
Not playing a STRIKE grants +2 ENERGY next turn. Killing the first enemy each round Draws N cards.
Remove CHARGE DRAW reduction effects. See the incoming enemy Intents' Values.
Signature BACKBONE cards gain an extra +3 Value. The Hearth can carry only 2 Artefacts at once.

character/items/curios.html.title: CURIOS
character/items/curios.html.description:
CURIOS are personal narrative items or unique finds. CURIOS can influence one Ember at a time, changing the way their keywords or cards perform as a whole. Curios can occupy Tool slots at a 3-1 exchange ratio.
Grants Your specific keyword PRIMARY cards a Tertiary keyword like DISCARD, DRAW, RETAIN, TRUSTY.
Ex. Your STRIKE cards now also have DRAW on them, without the extra ENERGY cost.
Grants Your specific keyword PRIMARY cards a Secondary keyword like AREA, LIGHTNING, MERCILESS, PIERCE, VAMPIRIC
Ex. Your COUNTER cards now also have LIGHTNING on them, without the extra ENERGY cost. Changes which Prism a specific Keyword scales with.
Ex. Your MOMENTUM cards now use GUILE instead of MIGHT. Halve a card's cost (rounded up).

character/items/tools.html.title: TOOLS
character/items/tools.html.description:
UTILITIES - items aimed at providing a roleplaying aid. Light Sources - provide vision in dark areas as long as they're burning. Torch Lantern Rations - necessary to regain Vitals during a Rest. Compass - inquiry about location. Will / Soul Compass Map - inquiry about location. Watch - inquiry about the time. Binoculars - for viewing things at a distance. Radio - for saying things at a distance. Spare Clothes - social decency. Soap / other Grooming supplies - social decency. Chalk - marking. Telescoping survey pole - checks ledges, floor weakness, water depth, trapped seams, or distance to a surface without committing a body. Hand crank pump - extracts fuel, coolant, blood, sludge, or water from wrecks or tanks Divining Bones - rituals, superstitions. Clamps - used to fasten things together. Can of Sealant - used for patching leaks or cracks. Oath Tokens - given when first incorporated into a Hearth, can grant entry to some places, and quite the opposite in others. ADVANTAGES - items aimed at BS ROLLS (CHECKS) benefits. Lockpicks - Reroll up to Half your rolled dice when attempting to open a lock. Crowbar - +2 Successes when attempting to open something that's locked. Glass cutter - Add +1 to all your rolled faces when cutting glass. Medical Kit - Remove 1 card added to the deck via the Trauma rule. Needs a Rest, uses up the Medical Kit. Stimulants - Improve one's reflexes, perception, or capabilities for one check.
Coffee +3 Dice rolled on INSIGHT checks.
Painkillers +3 Dice rolled on MIGHT checks.
Heroin +3 Dice rolled on GUILE checks.
Bond-Blood Transfusion - restore Half of the Hearth's VITALS. Can only take one between Rests. Forged Documents - Useful when trying to pass off as someone else. Bribe Money - Useful when dealing with officials.
Accelerant - Consume to gain 3 CHARGE charges.
GATEKEEPS - items required to even attempt certain BS ROLLS (CHECKS) Rope / Grappling hook - Allow climbing walls, cliffs, and crossing chasms. Hammer & Spikes - Allow rapid architecture. (Boarding up a door / window, fixing a hole) Vehicle Repair Kit - Allow tinkering with, repairing, and stealing vehicles.
Face Wrap / Mask - Disable the Madness mechanic when traversing the MIST. Official Permits - Allow access to restricted areas. Fuel - Allows running vehicles, generators, and machinery. Breaching Charge - Open most basic doors instantly, allow attempting to open heavy / secure doors.

bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.description: A tile containing either a Key (used to unlock NODE - DOOR), TOOLS, or CURIOS, or even ARTEFACTS.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/classical-soc.title: CLASSICAL-SOCIETY
lore/world/cultural-pillar/classical-soc.description: Time. Frontline soldiers. Resources in meatgrinders. Souls - the ones sent into battle and between folders and forms alike. Cities. Command continuity. Industry. Borders. National morale. Rigid hierarchy, layered bureaucracy. General staff for grand strategy. Trench officers enforce doctrine. Logistics commissars ration steel. Political overseers tie service to citizenship. "Stability through endurance." Lines held today secure tomorrow's world. Sacrifice carves victory from time. Balance tempers fire and machine. Conservative, rule-heavy; service grants citizenship. Glorifies patience, sacrifice via memorials. Slow-moving bureaucracy insulates homefront. Identity via regiment or home trench. Marriages arranged for stable lineages. Court-martials and executions for insubordination, both in the trench and on the streets. Rationed output: shells, food, conscripts. Trade alliances for raw ore. War bonds fund the grind. Trench sentries map Husk patrols via flares. Artillery barrages on swarm patterns. HOLD THE LINE: bunkers, wire, massed guns. Artillery softens, infantry anchors. Countercharges reclaim lost earth. Rotations prevent collapse. Stagnation (doctrinal rigidity). Attrition burnout (manpower drain). Supply vulnerabilities. lore/world/cultural-pillar/diesel-societ.title: DIESEL-SOCIETY
lore/world/cultural-pillar/diesel-societ.description: Empathy. Autonomy. Sometimes truth. Individual machines. Human middle-management. Leadership cadre. Data archives. Continuity (uninterrupted production lines). Diesel reserves (lifeblood). Core directives. Centralized, abstracted, distant. High algorithms/directorate oversee strategy. Factory-overseers manage servant production. Field coordinators for droid legions. Humans insulated; dissent rerouted to simulations. "War can be solved, eventually." Machines endure so people thrive. Diesel autonomy frees humanity from chains. Data proves iterative victory inevitable. Insulated comfort; servants handle labour/war. Technocratic surveillance ensures efficiency. Risk-averse elite; failures blamed on "glitches." Identity via directive affiliation (e.g., Sector-7 Citizen). Pairings optimized by compatibility algorithms. Exile as "decommissioning" - reassignment to fringes. Diesel/steel barter; factories as currency hubs. Surplus servants traded for rare fuels. Self-sustaining loops minimize human input. Swarm droid waves overwhelm foes. Heavy diesel constructs (tanks, walkers) for breakthroughs. A specialised unit for any situation. Autonomous adaptations mid-battle via uplinks. Civilian safety paramount evac them first. Diesel dependency - supply lines. Centralization bottleneck. Servant glitches from overuse. Elite detachment breeds resentment. lore/world/cultural-pillar/gm_guide.html.title: GM_Guide
lore/world/cultural-pillar/gm_guide.html.description:
People?
Souls?
Land?
Time?
Truth?
This is the most defining question.
Infrastructure?
Culture?
Leadership?
Knowledge?
The dead?
This determines how they fight, and what they fight for.
Central command?
Local officers?
Autonomous units?
Ritual specialists?
Machines?
This shapes governance and combat style.
Defensive necessity
Religious mandate
Historical grievance
Economic survival
“It’s already too late to stop”
This informs propaganda, education, and civilian life.Because war never ended, military logic leaks into civilian life.Examples:
Trench states have ration cards, curfews, and long-term planning ministries
Ember states have youth conscription, fast promotions, and normalized death
Technocratic states have civilian oversight that means nothing
This makes politics legible to players.They can feel where they are.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/magic-society.title: MAGIC-SOCIETY
lore/world/cultural-pillar/magic-society.description: Individuals. Souls. Long-term sanity. Static traditions. Weak bloodlines. Magic. Controlled territories. Sacrifice reserves. Cinders. Youthful vigor Decentralized, merit-based, brutal. Spell-weavers hold battlefield command. Cinders manage ritual authority and provide prophetic guidance. No eternal rulers. Tenures end in burnout or challenge. Leadership shifts with magical potency. "Better to burn fast than be erased slowly." The Mist devours the timid; fire claims the bold. Cinder magic is the world's true pulse - stasis invites Hollowing. Every flare extends humanity's defiant light. Fatalistic youth-heavy society. Reveres those who burn brightly. Conscription from adolescence; training in pain tolerance. Relationships forged in ritual circles, dissolved by burnout. These states normalize Hollowing. Identity tied to cinder-mark (personal magic scar, acquired or branded on at birth). Marriages as power alliances (merging lines of powerful casters). Trade in crystals, soul-essences, scrolls. Opportunistic raids on Husk clusters for magical reagents. Currency secondary to raw potency (measured in burn-rate) Aggressive flare-strikes, never prolonged defenses. Hit-and-burn: ignite, retreat, repeat. Drag foes into cinder-zones for mutual Hollowing. Weaponize burnout with suicide spells as last resort. Burnout epidemics (mass Hollowing cascades). Youth dependency, little age-granted wisdom. Internal purges (challenges fracture unity). bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.title: NODE - RITUAL
bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.description:
Gain N shards, but take a random status into the deck. Gain access to a rare Curio or Artefact, but lose time / trigger a hostile encounter.

bs-(between-stabbings)/talk_the_talk.htm.title: TALK_THE_TALK
bs-(between-stabbings)/talk_the_talk.htm.description:
Affinity (1-10) is the long-term relationship state, hidden from players and used as the underlying modifier. Affinity can be earned or lost depending on player actions and/or reputation. Each of the examples given can also be reversed to achieve the opposite effect. It usually moves in increments of 1-2 points at a time, dependent on campaign length.
Positive Actions: Fulfilling an NPC's request (ex. Doing the job you're hired for)
Siding with them in an argument (ex. Helping the barman rid of an out of control drunk)
Doing something they benefit from (ex. Shutting down a local mafioso that was threatening them - Important! The Players do not need to know how and who they're benefitting when taking an action to still be viewed positively) Negative Actions: Siding with the opposite side of the argument (ex. Joining the aforementioned drunkard)
Stealing from or harming the NPC directly (ex. Injuring the barman when a fight erupts between patrons)
Doing something that affects them negatively (ex. letting a cult blow up their house by being late to the scene - Important! The NPC has to know about something to be upset over it) Patience is a derived scene clock: Affinity × an NPC/situation-specific multiplier (1-5). This multiplier is entirely in the hands of the GM, but as a rough reference guide: 1–2: distracted, busy, hostile environment
3: normal conversation
4–5: relaxed, safe, or invested Players do not see the scores themselves. They only get narrative clues, shifts in tone, body language, and response quality. Insight can dampen Patience damage, so socially skilled characters can prolong or soften conversations. When Patience runs out, the NPC ends the exchange, refuses further pressure, or forces the scene to close. Base Patience damage per investigative question/request: 6 Minor provocation / repetitive pressure: 9 Major push / insult / contradiction: 12
Insight dampens this DMG by its raw value, but cannot reduce it below 1.
Once per exchange, when the players introduce new, relevant leverage / a meaningful concession / impactful information, the tone of the conversation may shift. If the leverage is significant, Patience loss from the next interaction is reduced (bring the DMG down one level 12->9->6->1), and the NPC’s response changes accordingly.
Repeated or low-value attempts have no effect, or may even insult the NPC. A pivot must do at least one of these: Change the stakes (“If you don’t help, this gets worse for you too”) Reveal something relevant (not trivia, actual leverage) Offer real cost (risk, debt, obligation—not pocket change) Reframe the situation (make the NPC reconsider their position)
When Patience would reach 0, the players may attempt one final appeal: [offer leverage / make a meaningful concession]. If the offer is compelling, the NPC allows one additional exchange and may adjust Affinity [positively / negatively]. If it fails, the conversation ends immediately and Affinity is reduced.
The offer must be one of:
Value (money, item, favour)
Information (useful, relevant, new)
Commitment (promise with cost or risk)
If it’s not one of those, it’s just stalling.
NPC's can also receive Quirks if they're relevant enough or just appear often in a game or its segment. These are additional modifiers to the rules above so use them sparingly.
As a Player, feel free to add nuance to your character by borrowing some of these, maybe even in combination!

character/character-creation.html.title: CHARACTER CREATION
character/character-creation.html.description:
Start with a concept: who is this character, and what do they do in the world? Pick an Oath: this sets your core Prisms (MIGHT, GUILE, INSIGHT) and starting deck identity.
Build your starting deck: use the listed Oath deck, then apply any allowed starter options, shards, or oath-specific choices. (OATH_OF_THE_PYRE OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER)
Choose your CHARGE.
Optional: add personality using Quirks from the TALK_THE_TALK for inspiration, especially if you want extra flavour beyond the mechanics. Write a short backstory for the GM, in collaboration with the other players, explaining how you're bonded, unless it is pre-specified in the hook. Oprtional: If you're playing a One-shot, or a shorter campaign, it is likely you will be using a 3-2-1 Prism distribution instead of the standard 2-1-0 +1.
OATH_OF_THE_PYRE
Passive: MEND MIGHT VITALS at combat end.
CHARGE: Sacrifice up to MIGHT VITALS, gain as much BRACE, and 2 X MOMENTUM
Prism Scores: 2 GUILE, 1 INSIGHT, 3 MIGHT. In‑World Occupation Forrest works as an Ember mortician with Controlled Release Services, preparing the unstable and terminal for safe venting and disposal in city wards. Backstory He survived a ward failure that should have turned the whole building into a scorching crater, calmly moving patients into improvised cooling rigs and keeping collapse to a minimum while others panicked. Since then, commanders quietly pass his name along whenever a district needs someone who will stay in the room until the dust settles. Role in the Hearth
At the table, Forrest is the Hearth’s self‑sacrificing stabilizer, spending VITALS to bulk up BRACE and stock MOMENTUM, then patching the group with MEND at combat end. In roleplay, he is the one who assumes the ugly burdens, stepping into danger with the expectation that his Hearth will turn his spent health and quiet reliability into a lasting advantage.
OATH_OF_THE_PYRE
Passive: At the end of each combat round, gain +1 MOMENTUM.
CHARGE: STRIKE with MOMENTUM multiplier (DMG = STRIKE X MOMENTUM, instead of STRIKE + MOMENTUM).
Prism Scores: 2 GUILE, 1 INSIGHT, 3 MIGHT. In‑World Occupation Jessie serves as a trench courier, sprinting between bunkers and battlelines to relay orders and mark shifting lines. Backstory On one infamous night they ran the trench's length three times, carrying corrections that stopped an allied regiment from being shelled by its own compatriots, and each run was somehow faster than the last. Role in the Hearth
Mechanically, Jessie is a tempo engine who converts steady, reliable gain of MOMENTUM into a single overwhelming CHARGE that multiplies STRIKE instead of merely adding to it. In roleplay, Jessie sets the rhythm of fights, constantly urging the Hearth to keep the sequence going so that when they finally call for their big move, everyone has already helped load that decisive hit
OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT
Passive: Your IGNITE effects do not decay.
CHARGE: IGNITE INSIGHT Distribute the stacks as you wish. Already IGNITEd enemies take their total IGNITE stacks as DMG immediately (Does not remove stacks).
Prism Scores: 1 GUILE, 3 INSIGHT, 2 MIGHT. In‑World Occupation Lantern grid technician in a dense city, maintaining the web of ward‑lights that keep the Grey Mist thin in alleys and lower city levels. Backstory Arthur once stabilized an entire district’s lantern network by chaining his own resonance through faulty cables, turning himself into a continuous generator that stopped Hollows from drifting in. Once he lights something, he is obsessed with keeping that flame “on spec” instead of letting it burn out. Role in the Hearth
Mechanically, Arthur leans on INSIGHT by making IGNITE effects persistent and then redistributing those stacks with his CHARGE, including sudden bursts of damage to enemies that are already burning. In roleplay, he treats enemies and hazards like nodes in a circuit, asking his Hearth to tag, corral, and revisit targets so his lingering fire can keep compounding while the others manoeuvre around his planned burn pattern.
OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT
Passive: Leftover GUARD is halved at turn end, instead of vanishing completely.
CHARGE: STRIKE = Missing VITALS
Prism Scores: 2 GUILE, 3 INSIGHT, 1 MIGHT. In‑World Occupation Clyde works as a private investigator in the city, taking on missing persons cases, quiet blackmail jobs, and disputes that the officials would rather avoid. Backstory He made his reputation by following cases everyone else had already written off, especially the ones that led through sealed alleys, and neighbourhoods where people preferred not to be seen answering questions. What set him apart was not charm or violence, but stubborn persistence and a habit of noticing what others walked right past. Role in the Hearth
Clyde’s mechanics reward taking hits in a controlled way, as his passive lets leftover GUARD persist and his CHARGE turns missing VITALS into a devastating STRIKE. In roleplay, he invites the Hearth to stand behind him when they cannot afford to fall, making it clear that every point of damage he soaks now will come back later when he finally swings with everything he has lost
OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER
Passive: Discard the first negative STATUS drawn (by You) each turn without triggering its effects.
CHARGE: Reduce the cost of all cards currently dealt to 1 this turn.
Prism Scores: 3 GUILE, 2 INSIGHT, 1 MIGHT. In‑World Occupation Kay is a corridor smuggler who specializes in leading groups through half‑lit, Mist‑choked routes that official patrols consider too unstable to risk. Backstory She earned her name by always finding a way through. When one path was sealed, she came back with two more, and when the straight route failed, she knew the side door, the roof line, the drain passage, or the gap between buildings that nobody else had thought to try. That instinct for alternate routes and urban escape lines is what makes her great at slipping through the city, unstoppable. Role in the Hearth
Kay’s toolkit leans on GUILE, discarding the first negative STATUS they draw each turn and using CHARGE to slash the ENERGY cost of cards currently in play down for a turn. In roleplay, they function as the Hearth’s mental filter and opportunist, steering the group away from psychic clutter while turning a single good turn of draws into a sudden storm of cheap, coordinated plays.
OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER
Passive: Dealing unblocked HP damage grants +1 CHARGE.
CHARGE: Stun an enemy. (Pause their Intent)
Prism Scores: 3 GUILE, 1 INSIGHT, 2 MIGHT. In‑World Occupation Pam is an alley enforcer who does quiet contract work for locals, tracking down volatile Embers, Cinders, and predators that threaten her crowded streets. Backstory She first made a name cleaning up after a failed ritual in a tenement block, hunting the surviving participants through stairwells and courtyards before their instability turned into a full collapse incident. To the people who live there, she is not an assassin or a saviour but something just morally grey enough in the middle of the two. Role in the Hearth
Pam’s mechanics reward precise, unblocked aggression, gaining CHARGE whenever she deals HP damage that gets through defenses and spending it to stun enemies, pausing their Intent. In roleplay, she serves as the Hearth’s opener and control piece, using quick, decisive strikes to set the pace of a fight and create windows where the others can commit to high‑risk plays while key threats are locked in place.

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/gotcha..title: GOTCHA
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/gotcha..description: It is essentially a hypothetical combat boast / contest over whether you can “outplay” another player’s line. When declaring a "Gotcha" the initiator is basically saying "If I played my hand against yours, I could damage You, without taking a hit myself". Uses actual CHARGE charges as chips. The point is to give excess charges a place to matter and to let ownership of charges shift between players in a dramatic way. The loser’s charges are transferred through to the winner. If the pot grows, enemies may flee: Flee chance = number of chips in the pot as a percentage, so 30 chips = 30% flee chance, per turn, not per enemy. Free opt-out if the "defender" has fewer cards than the initiator. That gives the minigame a clean threshold and makes hand size matter in a readable way. It also prevents the whole thing from becoming a mandatory duel every time someone says “Gotcha.” There is also a One-Gotcha-Per-Player-Per-Exploration suggestion to avoid just that.Starting bet low enough to matter but not so high that it is spammed. A good range: Start at 5 charges for a basic Gotcha. Start at 10 charges for a more serious challenge. Raise in increments of 5.   That keeps the whole thing readable and ensures the minigame is about confidence and momentum, not just who has the largest stockpile. One check per transfer (if the transfer is >10 charges), not one per 10 charges. So: when charges transfer, the winner makes one check total, the size of the transfer may modify that check, but it does not create multiple separate checks. Having two sides and letting the rest of the table pick sides turns Gotcha into a spectator bet instead of a private duel. One player challenges another. Other players can join either side. They contribute charges to the pool. The winner takes the pot, and the group gets the fun of social momentum around the exchange.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/nomad-society.title: NOMAD-SOCIETY
lore/world/cultural-pillar/nomad-society.description: Territory Settlements Outsiders Material surplus The weak who slow the caravan Vehicles (these are lineage, inheritance, identity) Bloodlines / crews Routes and path-knowledge Mobility itself Decentralized, vehicle-based Authority lies with: Lead drivers (navigation authority) Mechanics (survival authority) Path-readers / Mist-sensitives (mythic authority) No “king.” At best: Convoy leaders (temporary, situational) Leadership changes based on context: Travel = driver leads Breakdown = mechanic leads Danger = path-reader leads Authority shifts mid-scene depending on the problem. “Standing still is death.” “The world belongs to what moves through it.” “We don’t fight the Mist - we outlive it.” “Settlers choose their graves. We don’t.” No permanent homes → everything modular, collapsible, strapped down Children raised on the move, taught roles early Relationships are practical first, emotional second Identity = your vehicle + your crew Marriages = merging crews / resources Exile = being left behind (worse than death) Trade is opportunistic: salvage fuel parts information about routes Currency is secondary to utility They read: wind shifts in the Mist sound distortion Husk movement rhythms Engine rhythms used as signal patterns Horns, chants, or drumbeats to guide or disperse Husks Smoke mixtures that mask or redirect presence Path-readers are: respected feared possibly "other" Dependence on vehicles → breakdown = crisis Limited heavy infrastructure → can’t hold ground Internal fragmentation → clans may not cooperate Resource bottlenecks (fuel, parts, specialists) Optional nasty one: Aging problem → older members become liabilities if they can’t keep up The Ashwheel Convoy Ironwake Drift The Gutterline Clans Brassroad Nomads The Coilbound Procession Sprocket Mile Syndicates The Rust Atlas Caravans Gearwake Circles The Cinder Axle Union Varnish Road Tribes The Long Dust March Red Horizon Lines The Endless Switchback The Passing Mile Kin The Vanishing Route The Driftway Peoples The No-Return Convoy The Betweenroads The Passing Weather Clans The Borrowed Ground Travelers Children of the Open Engine The Unmoored Bloodlines Those Who Refuse Stillness The Last Moving Kin The Oath of No Place The Windborne Remnants The Surviving Mile The Hollowroad Families The Quiet Departure Clans The Ones Who Did Not Settle

cards/keywords/primary/bleed.html.title: BLEED
cards/keywords/primary/bleed.html.description:
Deal Value DMG at the end of enemy's turn, bypassing GUARD. If applied to a wounded enemy, double the BLEED value.
Can be prevented by Healing (even at max HP/Vitals.) The would-be-healed amount is removed from current BLEED stacks before restoring HP/VITALS
Scales with MIGHT

cards/keywords/primary/brace.html.title: BRACE
cards/keywords/primary/brace.html.description:
Pool of Value. When gaining GUARD you may expend any amount of stacks from the pool and add it to the total GUARD received. Pool decreases when HP damage is taken.
Depending on the SYNC-AXIS; Harmony - halve, rounding up. Discord - remove all stacks.
Scales with GUILE

cards/keywords/primary/counter.html.title: COUNTER
cards/keywords/primary/counter.html.description: Grants Value stacks. Deals Value DMG back when hit; halve stacks (round up) after each trigger. Scales with GUILE

cards/keywords/primary/empower.html.title: EMPOWER
cards/keywords/primary/empower.html.description: Adds Value to the numeric effect of the next card played. Scales with INSIGHT

cards/keywords/primary/guard.html.title: GUARD
cards/keywords/primary/guard.html.description: Grants Value Block - stopping incoming DMG. Scales with GUILE

cards/keywords/primary/ignite.html.title: IGNITE
cards/keywords/primary/ignite.html.description:
Deal 1 DMG at the end of the enemy's turn, then decrease stacks by 1. If Stacks reach 9, remove all stacks and deal 9 DMG immediately. Players may choose to Vent their Ignite stacks, taking DMG = no. Stacks cleared. This counts as DMG, interacting with VITALS and MOMENTUM/BRACE rules.
Scales with INSIGHT

cards/keywords/primary/mend.html.title: MEND
cards/keywords/primary/mend.html.description:
Restores Value Vitals. Can remove BLEED stacks before they deal damage.
Scales with INSIGHT

cards/keywords/primary/momentum.html.title: MOMENTUM
cards/keywords/primary/momentum.html.description:
Pool of Value. When dealing DMG with STRIKE you may expend any amount of stacks from the pool and add it to the STRIKE dealt. Pool decreases when HP damage is taken.
Depending on the SYNC-AXIS; Harmony - halve, rounding up. Discord - remove all stacks.
Scales with MIGHT

cards/keywords/primary/strike.html.title: STRIKE
cards/keywords/primary/strike.html.description: Deal Value DMG. Scales with MIGHT

cards/keywords/secondary/area.html.title: AREA
cards/keywords/secondary/area.html.description: Affects ALL enemies in a chosen row. MOMENTUM stacks are converted at half efficiency.

cards/keywords/secondary/lightning.html.title: LIGHTNING
cards/keywords/secondary/lightning.html.description: Causes the played card to re-trigger once. If the card requires targeting, you make the choice each time. Cards with LIGHTNING cost +1 extra energy to play (so a total +2 cost increase when adding this to a card, instead of the usual +1).

cards/keywords/secondary/merciless.html.title: MERCILESS
cards/keywords/secondary/merciless.html.description:
If target has no Block, add 2xValue to the card's total Value.
Scales with Your highest Prism.

cards/keywords/secondary/pierce.html.title: PIERCE
cards/keywords/secondary/pierce.html.description: Ignores Block. Allows targeting of backrow (2nd line) enemies even if they're behind an enemy.

cards/keywords/secondary/vampiric.html.title: VAMPIRIC
cards/keywords/secondary/vampiric.html.description: Restore VITALS equal to 1/2 Value when hitting HP. Stacks.

cards/keywords/tertiary/discard.html.title: DISCARD
cards/keywords/tertiary/discard.html.description: Discard 1 card(s). Cards discarded this way give CHARGE charges equal to their cost (as if they were played), to their Owner.

cards/keywords/tertiary/exhaust.html.title: EXHAUST
cards/keywords/tertiary/exhaust.html.description:
Cards with this keyword are removed from the deck after being played. They can be restored at a NODE - REST at the cost of 1 SHARDS. Since they remove a card from play, they count against the Minimum Deck limit, as if being removed for the counting.
Reminder: Cannot have less than 4 BACKBONE cards in your deck, meaning that 6/10 (for example) cards in your deck can be Exhaustible, if you wish so.

cards/keywords/tertiary/retain.html.title: RETAIN
cards/keywords/tertiary/retain.html.description:
Card stays in hand if unplayed. It also Persists in hand across combats.
Reminder: The hand limit is 5 cards

cards/keywords/unplayable.html.title: UNPLAYABLE
cards/keywords/unplayable.html.description: Cards with this keyword cannot be played, only Discarded or Exhausted by the effects of other cards, CHARGE, or other means.

cards/status_cards/ash.html.title: ASH
cards/status_cards/ash.html.description:
RETAIN
Cost: 2 Energy. EXHAUST when played.

cards/status_cards/doubt.html.title: DOUBT
cards/status_cards/doubt.html.description:
RETAIN UNPLAYABLE
At the end of turn, lose 1 CHARGE charge.

cards/status_cards/erosion.html.title: EROSION
cards/status_cards/erosion.html.description: Cost: 1 Energy. When drawn, take 1 DMG. DRAW 1 card when played.

cards/status_cards/hollow.html.title: HOLLOW
cards/status_cards/hollow.html.description: Cost: 1 Energy. No effect. Persists between combats. Dissipates on its own during a LEVEL_UP/NODE - REST

cards/status_cards/infection.html.title: INFECTION
cards/status_cards/infection.html.description:
UNPLAYABLE.
While in hand all Vitals Restoring effects (MEND, VAMPIRIC) deal DMG instead.
*(This does mean you can damage enemies using your MEND cards, and take unavoidable DMG from your VAMPIRIC cards)

cards/status_cards/injury.html.title: INJURY
cards/status_cards/injury.html.description:
Cost: 2 Energy. RETAINEXHAUST.
While in hand, your CHARGE is UNPLAYABLE.

cards/status_cards/mist.html.title: MIST
cards/status_cards/mist.html.description: RETAIN UNPLAYABLE When drawn, shuffle a MIST into the Discard Pile.

cards/status_cards/panic.html.title: PANIC
cards/status_cards/panic.html.description:
RETAIN
Cost: 1 Energy. When drawn, DISCARD another card from your hand. It does not contribute to CHARGE Gauge when discarded this way.

cards/status_cards/trembling.html.title: TREMBLING
cards/status_cards/trembling.html.description:
Cost: 2 Energy. EXHAUST.
While in hand, playing another card deals Self DMG = ENERGY cost of the card played.

cards/status_cards/wound.html.title: WOUND
cards/status_cards/wound.html.description:
UNPLAYABLE
When drawn, take 2 PIERCE DMG.

cards/backbone.html.title: BACKBONE
cards/backbone.html.description: Starter Cards with only One Keyword, they have an innate +3 Value. Backbone cards are limited to the single-keyword cards provided in the Starting Oath Deck. Adding any keyword or modifying them turns them into Custom cards, removing the +3 bonus. Likewise, removing extra Keywords from a CUSTOMS card brings it back to a Backbone once its left with just One Keyword. Deck Rule: A player’s deck must always contain at least 4 Backbone Cards, as detailed below.

cards/customs.html.title: CUSTOMS
cards/customs.html.description:
Custom Cards have more than One Keyword. Achieved through adding keywords to BACKBONE cards.
Card ENERGY Cost Scales with Keyword count (1:1), making complex cards more expensive to play, but more powerful in return.
They use only the related Prism's Value for any scaling effects, without the BACKBONE Reliability Bonus. If a card demands targeting for more than one of its keywords, each keyword is "aimed" individually by the player, when resolving the card.

cards/status.html.title: STATUS
cards/status.html.description:
STATUS cards are Negative effects applied by Enemies, Events, Reshuffles, CURSED, or through losing VITALS. Unless noted otherwise (by having their ENERGY cost listed), these are UNPLAYABLE, and disappear at the end of combat (with the exception of HOLLOW, and statuses added via VITALS loss).
STATUS cards are ownerless, and so are dealt out randomly from left to right starting from players with the least amount of STATUS cards in hand.

character/oaths/conduit/charges.html.title: CHARGES
character/oaths/conduit/charges.html.description:
Enemy loses all GUARD and COUNTER stacks.
STRIKE = Missing VITALS
Play up to INSIGHT cards for free, if they scale with INSIGHT (from other players too).
IGNITE INSIGHT Distribute the stacks as you wish. Already IGNITEd enemies take their total IGNITE stacks as DMG immediately (Does not remove stacks).
Add current BRACE and MOMENTUM together to one of the two, then gain INSIGHT of the other. Push/Pull an enemy to a different Row, Advance their Intent (skip the current one in favour of the next).

character/oaths/mistwalker/charges.html.title: CHARGES
character/oaths/mistwalker/charges.html.description:
Reduce the cost of all cards currently dealt to 1 this turn. Next card played this turn takes effect 2 extra times (so 3 activations total). Stun an enemy. (Pause their Intent) Draw GUILE cards, regain 1 ENERGY, EXHAUST all STATUS cards currently dealt.
ALL played COUNTER also acts as STRIKE with equal Value this turn.
STRIKE = 2 DMG. Multiplied by 2x for every STATUS currently dealt.

character/oaths/pyre/charges.html.title: CHARGES
character/oaths/pyre/charges.html.description:
Sacrifice up to MIGHT VITALS, gain as much BRACE, and 2 X MOMENTUM
Gain GUARD = MIGHT X N (Player Count)
STRIKE with MOMENTUM multiplier (DMG = STRIKE X MOMENTUM, instead of STRIKE + MOMENTUM).
MEND Half Max VITALS, grant MIGHT CHARGE charges to another Ember.
STRIKE ALL Front enemies, BLEED ALL Back enemies.
Pause up to MIGHT enemy Intents. Lose 3 ENERGY for every Intent Paused this way.

character/oaths/charge.html.title: CHARGE
character/oaths/charge.html.description:
Each player has an individual Ember Gauge. Building up: Play: +1 Charge per 1 ENERGY spent on your cards.
Sacrifice: +2 Charges per 1 ENERGY cost of a card willingly discarded from hand. You still must pay the card's cost to discard it, but it has no effect unless described otherwise. Charges can stack up indefinitely. Activation:
Costs 10 Charges. Every subsequent activation during one EXPLORATION costs +5 Charges for that particular Ember's Charge. After Charging, the Hearth draws 1 Card less next turn. Subsequent Charges (even from the same Character) in the same turn, add a stacking +1 to this penalty. (*So a second Charge makes the penalty 1 + 1+1. Then the third increases it to 1 + 1+1 + 1+1+1, etc.) Additional Effects: Every 10 Charges stored, gain +1 to the values of ALL your scalable Cards. However, every time you increase this bonus, roll 1d100. Rolling a number lower than your current Charges causes you to take 1d6 DMG for every 10 Charges held.

ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/hollows.ht.title: HOLLOWS
ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/hollows.ht.description: These creatures emerge from the carcasses of HUSKS once they grow cold. They are impossible shadowy silhouettes that move and act in unusual ways, already detached from any humanity. They may take on appearance of people, and even confine their forms with clothing, but make no mistake, these are not attempts to fit in society, but merely conceal themselves for the hunt.

ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/husks.html.title: HUSKS
ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/husks.html.description: These are the usual effects of EROSION and soon-to-follow Collapse. They are the most human, which makes them all the more horrifying to encounter. Often appearing in groups, as one person's Collapse is likely to cause a chain reaction within a crowd. When a soul shifts over the edge of its integrity, the skin burns and melts almost like wax, often creating uncanny, or only human-resemblant forms once it settles.

ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/loops.html.title: LOOPS
ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/loops.html.description:
BOXES: Red - STRIKE (aimed at the Hearth)
Blue - GUARD (Usually on itself, but full HP enemies can guard Damaged enemies) Yellow - Conditions (Placed alongside the original Intent) Can also mean Debuffs.
Grey/Green - STATUS Cards (shuffled into the shared deck immediately) LINES symbolise adapting patterns which change the next intent (after the one currently shown): Red - If hit Blue - if took no DMG last turn. Grey: standard progression **Encounter Templates Use composition, not scaling. Skirmish (~60%) – quick and risky (death favours those who wait). Annoyance (~20%) – awkward pressure (backline pests). Attrition (~15%) – force reshuffles. Wildcard/Boss (~5%) – break the rules. GM rule of thumb: Party handles ~N+1 enemy actions per round comfortably.

ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/phenomena..title: PHENOMENA
ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/phenomena..description: Phenomena are individual Turned that break the norm, their Collapse either more sudden or powerful, or both. As such, their forms are more erratic and visibly distinct, causing all the more terror when witnessing such an abnormality. While their corporeal forms may resemble that of HUSKS or HOLLOWS, their heads are almost always replaced with a supernatural phenomenon, hinting at the unusual nature of these beings.

ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/reinforcem.title: REINFORCEMENTS
ds-(during-stabbings)/enemies/reinforcem.description: The Topmost row of enemies are the Reinforcements. These enemies cannot yet act, and cannot be targeted. As soon as an enemy in front of them perishes, the Reinforcement from the same column drops down, as if affected by gravity. Enemies may be added into the Reinforcements row as part of enemy LOOPS or as part of the encounter itself.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/religion-phil.title: COLLAPSE
lore/world/cultural-pillar/religion-phil.description: Stage 1: "The Burnups" Chronic fever, ash coughing Veins, eyes, fingernails, hair ends light up Heightened Emotional states Fire responds to your presence Stage 2: Burst One moment too much: A stored surge A panicked release A battlefield spike The affected spontaneously combusts, and as the fire dies down, Memory, identity, intent - gone, into the GREY MIST. Stage 3: Hollowing (fractions of a second after Breach) Organs char Blood thickens into slag Nerves fire randomly from thermal stress The heart becomes a smouldering knot Skin cracks like fired clay The person is already gone. Stage 4: The Husk Walks A Husk: Avoids nothing Moves toward thermal gradients like crowds, fires, battlefields Why? Because residual soul-heat seeks other heat to equalize with. Husks drift toward the living, or at the very least towards where they reside. That’s why they’re exterminated on sight.

lore/world/cultural-pillar/religion-phil.title: FAITHS
lore/world/cultural-pillar/religion-phil.description:
Deities, or deity-like entities are believed to exist, but there's scant worthwhile evidence besides faith and the burning need for a truth to cling onto. As such many beliefs have emerged after the Great War, and the GREY MIST came...
Many peoples believe that CINDERS themselves are worthy of worship, even going as far as to sacrifice themselves for these "Prophets" and "Immortals" as they call the rogue Embers.

Mentioned separately in the THE GREAT HEARTH note, these cultists sometimes refer to a being that's unnervingly consistent between reports. They speak of a being with three eyes, and a heart floating between its horns, beating in a steady, calm rhythm, as small streaks of blood paint the air around it in a circular pattern. This bloody halo is said to be the inspiration for the cult's doctrine and end goal...
A belief shared mostly by battlefield veterans, who swear the mist can literally devour people and things, insisting its not just as a metaphor for "losing sight of something"...

lore/world/cultural-pillar/heroic-societ.title: HEROIC-SOCIETY
lore/world/cultural-pillar/heroic-societ.description: Regular troops. Equipment budgets. Failed/Old prototypes. Conscripts' comfort. National pride/symbols. Prestige tech. Heroic exceptions. Propaganda machine. Adaptive doctrine. Split: mass-mobilization commanders for hordes. Heroic exceptions for superweapon ops. Populist high command (voted in on victory promises). Tech-overseers. Authority rotates with results—failures demote fast. "One decisive breakthrough will end this." Every martyr's wave buys time for the superweapon. Heroes prove the horde's unbreakable spirit. Learning from mistakes hones the winning edge. Propaganda-saturated, populist fervour. Hero worship; quick canonization of dead aces. Mass rallies double as recruitment drives. Forgets rank-and-file dead; elevates the exceptional. Identity via banner affiliation or hero cult. Marriages incentivized for breeding cannon fodder. Exile as "banner-stripping"—public shaming. Total war footing: farms feed troops, factories churn prototypes. Trade in scrap from failed mechs, hero relics. Barter over currency; loyalty points for service Overwhelming human waves pin enemies. Superweapons (mechs, beams) for kill shots—glorious even in failure. Diversion tactics: feint with fodder, strike with prototypes. Post-battle reviews adapt for next wave. Gear shortages cripple horde effectiveness. Superweapon unreliability (budget black holes). Morale crashes on repeated prototype flops. headers.heading: 2. What Must Be Preserved at All Costs?

lore/world/physical-pillar/cities.html.title: CITIES

lore/world/vibe.html.title: VIBE
lore/world/vibe.html.description:
Ember Deck is a dieselpunk low‑fantasy setting: think late‑1920s/1930s industry, artillery, engines, and trench maps, but soaked in ash, bad weather, and magical fallout. Magic is everywhere, yet almost nobody uses it well; most people can maybe spark a torch or vent a small surge, while precise, repeatable spellwork is rare and risky. The default tone is dark and gritty: the world is miserable to live in, people assume the worst, and survival choices often matter more than ideals.
The Great War never truly stopped; it shattered into endless local wars over resources and defensible ground. Borders, regimes, and even school maps keep changing every few years as frontlines creep back and forth, so people trust their city, their unit, or their convoy far more than any abstract “nation.” Travel beyond a well‑lit Hearth is frightening even for trained Embers because the GREY MIST, Hollows, and roaming armies make the world outside feel half‑lost and actively hostile.In‑world, people talk about “magic,” but underneath it behaves more like fallout from an overtaxed soul‑furnace than like a clean spell system. The first world‑scale war came early, killed staggering numbers of people, and overloaded Hell, which in this setting is a processing furnace for souls rather than a moral destination. When the furnace could not keep up, the overflow bled back into the world as raw, dangerous soul‑energy; that leak is what people now experience as magic.
Because of that origin, magic behaves more like radiation or pressure than like a tame tool. It builds up in living bodies, causing telltale signs like glowing veins and eyes, fever, and cracked skin, and if it is not safely vented or shared through a Hearth, it can trigger collapse into a Hollow. Anyone can coax a small effect into existence, but pushing for more power without training, support, and cooling is like stepping into a reactor core: the strength is there, but precision and survival are not.The GREY MIST is the other half of the disaster: it is the cold, impersonal “will” of all that unprocessed soul‑stuff leaking into reality. It does not carry feelings, only fragments of memory and belief, and it clings to low‑light, low‑heat areas; that is why cities burn lanterns even at noon and why darkness is treated as a safety hazard, not an aesthetic choice. Walking into thick Mist can mean hallucinations, personality bleed, and temporary loss of self as your mind is pressed against countless other impressions.
When a person’s internal load of soul‑energy spikes too far, they do not explode outward; instead, their “soul container” implodes and fails. That process strips away memory and identity into the Mist and leaves behind a Hollow: a thermally vitrified, zombie‑like shell that shambles toward heat and life because residual soul‑heat seeks equilibrium. Hollows are exterminated on sight not because they are evil, but because everyone knows they were people yesterday and that collapse can happen to anyone whose system fails.Player characters are Embers, people whose spark flared bright enough that they can work significant magic but at the cost of burning through their own lifespan. To survive, most Embers are bound into Hearths: tightly linked groups whose soul‑bond spreads the heat and pressure across multiple people, allowing safer venting, shared emotional regulation, and somewhat cleaner channelling of power. The bond shapes both personality and combat style, and mechanically it affects shared energy, damage mitigation, and how well the group fights together.When a Hearth bond forms, it may be due to ritual, traumatic shared experience, or long, intense closeness; over time, members often lose or blur their memories of who they were before, which weakens traditional ideas of a fixed “self.” If a bond breaks catastrophically and an Ember survives, they become a Cinder: a lone, unstable caster with power but no damping, prone to predation on others or complete mental break. Cinders make excellent antagonists because they know they are burning out and often respond by consuming captives as fuel or lashing out at the world that failed them.States, cities, and factions treat Hearths, Embers, and even Cinders as weapons first and everything else second. Military service is universal but intentionally short; strategists learned that extended deployment makes people Hollow out faster, so they rotate bodies through the grinder to extract usefulness before collapse. With the Great War fragmented into local conflicts, each resource‑rich or defensible location becomes a prize, and everyone expects conscription, propaganda, and some level of normalized atrocity as part of everyday life.
For players, this means the default expectation is not clean heroism. Characters are allowed—often encouraged—to be compromised: noble in some ways and selfish in others, more concerned with keeping their Hearth alive, securing a working rail line, or preventing a Cinder from devouring a village than with “saving the world.” The setting supports investigative missions, deniable operations, front‑line actions, and dirty survival work where magic feels potent but dangerous, the world pushes back hard, and every victory leaves scars.
