bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.title: NODE - PREDATOR
bs-(between-stabbings)/exploration/node-.description:
Predator Encounters have their Domains, which are always an area of 7 hexes, but the shape of them is entirely map-dependent (think of water filling a container). The Domain outline, and the Predator, is drawn at the start of the exploration, for transparency (and lack of a better method of map presentation as of right now).Predator Encounters will activate once the players step on any of their Domain Nodes, and will follow them until successful, or cut off by a NODE - UNSTABLE.
Predator Encounters move 1 space after the group finishes moving, and 2 spaces after a NODE - REST, NODE - RITUAL, or NODE - REWARD.
If a NODE - PREDATOR ends its move on a Node adjacent to the group, Combat is initiated.

cards/keywords/tertiary/cursed.html.title: CURSED
cards/keywords/tertiary/cursed.html.description: Adding a CURSED Keyword to a card makes it add a random STATUS card into the shared deck when played, But, it reduces the card's overall cost by 2 ENERGY (effectively -1 reduction as CURSEDare still technically keywords and so they increase a card's cost by 1).

cards/status_cards/madness.html.title: MADNESS
cards/status_cards/madness.html.description:
UNPLAYABLE RETAIN
When drawn, place the card from your hand onto your EMBER SHEET. Your Passive is locked while it remains there. This card will stay on your sheet until the next reshuffle, or combat end, whichever comes first.
Madness can be DISCARDed / EXHAUSTed by other cards/CHARGEs/effects.

character/oaths/conduit/conduit-passives.title: CONDUIT PASSIVES
character/oaths/conduit/conduit-passives.description:
Leftover GUARD is halved at turn end, instead of vanishing completely.
Your IGNITE effects do not decay.
Whenever two cards with matching keywords are played consecutively, the effect triggers once more, using Your raw INSIGHT score for Value.
Overhealing from any source grants the healer CHARGE equal to the amount overhealed.
First card played each turn costs -1 ENERGY.
EMPOWER INSIGHT the first card played each turn.

character/oaths/mistwalker/mistwalker-pa.title: MISTWALKER PASSIVES
character/oaths/mistwalker/mistwalker-pa.description:
Dealing unblocked HP damage grants +1 CHARGE.
Discard the first negative STATUS drawn (by You) each turn without triggering its effects.
Start each combat with GUILE BRACE and MOMENTUM.
Landing a final blow on an enemy lets you DRAW 1 Card OR regain 1 ENERGY.
At the Start of each of your turns, gain +1 BRACE.
First COUNTER played each turn reduces a chosen enemy's GUARD by the same value.

character/oaths/pyre/pyre-passives.html.title: PYRE PASSIVES
character/oaths/pyre/pyre-passives.html.description:
MEND MIGHT VITALS at combat end.
If Your STRIKE OR BLEED kills an enemy, all excess STRIKE damage OR BLEED stacks is/are applied the next (left to right) target.
Discarding for CHARGE charges grants You +3 instead of +2 charges per Card's ENERGY cost.
Whenever the Hearth takes VITALS damage, You gain +1 CHARGE Charge (Per Activation, not per DMG).
At the end of each combat round, gain +1 MOMENTUM.
Every time You kill an enemy in combat, gain a stacking +1 BLEED value this combat.

lore/in-world/personal/recovered-journal.title: Recovered Journal Page
lore/in-world/personal/recovered-journal.description:
12th July, 1941I did not stay to see the bodies.It sounds cowardly, I know, but there are things the body decides on before the mind has a chance to speak, and this sure was one of them.
One moment I'm standing there listening to a butcher argue over salt prices, and the next everyone was screaming like animals.Not all at once either.First came the sound.Like steam escaping a pipe.Then the smell.The man, I think it was a man, grabbed his own face before it happened. I remember that clearly because at first I thought he was laughing. His shoulders were shaking and people nearby stepped away thinking he was drunk.Then the light came through his fingers.Not around them.Through them.Orange first. Then white.I remember seeing the bones in his hands like candles inside paper lanterns.Someone shouted “COLLAPSE” and that was enough. The crowd folded in on itself immediately. People started climbing over market stalls before the blast even happened. Funny thing is I don’t think the explosion killed most of them. Panic did. Trampled each other without a second thought.The sound after the ignition is difficult to describe. Not loud. I expected loud. It was more like the air itself tearing open.I saw a woman’s coat catch fire without touching the flames.That was when I ran.I know what the papers will say tomorrow. Sudden ignition. Isolated instability event. Citizens are reminded to report signs of emotional agitation. Same old phrases.But I saw his eyes before it happened.There was no panic in them.Only exhaustion.And I think that frightens me more than the fire.

lore/in-world/personal/trench-journal-of.title: Trench Journal of Senior Fortification Engineer Armin Vale
lore/in-world/personal/trench-journal-of.description: Northern Front, Sector C-9Entry 218Six hours today.Maybe seven.Hard to tell anymore with the fog.The spotters rotate every two. Builders every four. Engineers stay until relieved because apparently we are less likely to “drift cognitively.” That is the phrase Command uses now. Drift cognitively...The Mist was calm today.That's bad.When it moves, at least you can track it. The drifting gives the eyes something to measure. But when it sits still beyond the wire you begin imagining shapes where there are none. Or worse.We lost another spotter last night.No screaming. No nothing. He just climbed over the trench wall during supper count and walked into the fog. Rifle still strapped over his shoulder like he was heading for patrol.Nobody followed him.Protocol says nobody follows them anymore.Corporal Mern says he saw someone standing in the Mist watching us afterward. Not a Husk. Too tall. Didn’t move right.Mern also hasn’t slept properly in eleven days, so that's something.Deployment ends next week.At least that is what they told us three weeks ago.The Mist just sits there, as do I.And every day it feels a little closer than yesterday.
lore/in-world/personal/trench-journal-of.sourcePath: LORE/In-World/Personal/Trench Journal of Senior Fortification Engineer Armin Vale.md

lore/in-world/redacted/high-priority-fro.title: HIGH PRIORITY FRONTLINE DISPATCH
lore/in-world/redacted/high-priority-fro.description:
To all active line officers stationed across the Northern Front:Reconnaissance confirms enemy reinforcement consolidation across sectors D-11 through H-2. Continued passive engagement will result in artillery enclosure within an estimated ██ days.As of 03:40 this morning, High Command has authorized Operation: GLASS LANTERN.All forward divisions are hereby ordered to initiate aggressive night advancement procedures against entrenched hostile positions.Gain sufficient ground to establish forward raiding corridors for continued nocturnal disruption campaigns.Force enemy rotation instability through sustained trench penetration.Projected casualty expectations remain within acceptable wartime thresholds.Officers are reminded:
hesitation under current conditions constitutes strategic sabotage.Mist density across no-man’s-land is expected to reach hazardous levels during the operation window. Ember detachments will therefore advance alongside standard infantry waves despite previous sustainability concerns.Rotations exceeding standard Collapse exposure thresholds have been temporarily authorized.Medical personnel are instructed to:
suppress panic responses,
maintain stimulant distribution,
report signs of pre-Collapse behaviour immediately.
Under no circumstances are retreat orders to be issued without direct authorization from Northern Command.The line must move forward.Even a kilometre.Especially a kilometre.Signed,
Marshal ████████
Northern Command Authority

lore/in-world/redacted/internal-incident.title: INTERNAL INCIDENT REPORT
lore/in-world/redacted/internal-incident.description:
At approximately 14:17 local time, officers stationed near the eastern produce lanes observed signs of an imminent Collapse Event within civilian crowds.Subject identified as:
████████████████
Male
Approximate age: 34-41
Employment records pending retrieval.Witnesses reported:
visible tremors,
facial distress,
uncontrolled thermal leakage from ocular cavities and fingertips.
Officer Halvern attempted standard containment procedure and issued evacuation orders approximately seven seconds before ignition.
The subject entered active Collapse state before suppression measures could be deployed.Effects included:
immediate thermal detonation,
localized ignition spread,
secondary panic surge,
trampling casualties,
infrastructural fire damage.
Total confirmed fatalities:
██
Missing:
████
Unrecoverable remains:
██████████Of note:
Several witnesses reported hearing the subject speaking prior to ignition. Statements vary heavily and are considered unreliable due to post-event distress.No evidence currently supports the existence of organized hostile involvement.Official public explanation issued:
Industrial chemical detonation caused by illegal heating equipment.
This explanation is to remain consistent across all press channels.Any mention of;
Collapse Events,
spontaneous ignition,
Husk manifestation,
resonance instability,
is classified under Emergency Silence Accord ██-C.
Further dissemination punishable by military tribunal.

lore/in-world/personal/diary-fragment.ht.title: Diary Fragment
lore/in-world/personal/diary-fragment.ht.description: 1st March, 1933We caught some sunlight today.Real sunlight.Not shellfire.Not signal flares.Just sunlight through the fog for maybe three, four seconds.Nobody spoke while it happened.Even the artillery seemed to quiet down for once.I think we all forgot it still existed.

lore/in-world/personal/scrap-of-paper-fo.title: Scrap of Paper Found Inside a Helmet
lore/in-world/personal/scrap-of-paper-fo.description: I think the worst part is that they still run like people.If they ran like animals maybe it would be easier.But they don’t.They stumble.They brace themselves on walls.Sometimes they protect their heads from falling debris.Like they remember being alive just enough to make this cruel.
lore/in-world/personal/scrap-of-paper-fo.sourcePath: LORE/In-World/Personal/Scrap of Paper Found Inside a Helmet.md

bs-(between-stabbings)/doctrines/a-guide.title: A Guide to Doctrines
bs-(between-stabbings)/doctrines/a-guide.description: At doctrine selection: choose 1 of 3 normally. OR Take a Burden Doctrine, which is an immediate downside, but, gain access to another doctrine choice (1 of 3) afterward. Some Doctrines are purely Passive effects, but others require a Check. When trying to activate a Doctrine, roll 2d6, and compare the roll's results to the DC. These Doctrine Checks can only be attempted once per scene A Scene is a single combat encounter, an exploration, or a Social encounter. It is worth differentiating that while combats and social interactions happen with the grand exploration map in the background, every time the players return to the hex grid it is treated as a new scene for this purpose. Basic, simple effects.Reliable and Moderate.Specialized.Powerful doctrines only.A one-in-a-session kind of effect. when making a character, (maybe in "advanced character creation", akin to the Quirks table) agree on one of the Societies. Your Hearth starts with +1 Allegiance with that society. to get a given society's cards into the pool you need at least 1 allegiance with them. At 3+ allegiance you get access to more rare and powerful doctrines from that society. Societies with <1 allegiance will have only their Burdens/Gambits doctrines in the main pool. Where your HQ/Hideout/Base of operations is, based on that settlements society, gain +1 allegiance with them. Works better in campaigns than one shots, as it allows the players to actually pick themselves. taking assignments from a given society gives +1 allegiance, fulfilling said assignment gives another +1, BUT failing or dropping the assignment will give a -2 (lose the initial +1 given and then lose 1 more) Important: since Gambit choices are available essentially always for every Society, they should be the most flavourful. A group not allegied with, say, Trench society should be able to get a Risky offer from them and think "hey these guys have a neat play style, let's engage with them more after this". ONLY through: Character origin Establishing HQ Accepting long-term assignments Completing major operations Formal alliances ONLY through: Betrayal Failed assignments Public opposition Supporting rivals Abandonment

bs-(between-stabbings)/doctrines/diesel-.title: DIESEL-DOCTRINES
bs-(between-stabbings)/doctrines/diesel-.description:
12 Gain 1 SHARDS.
After defeating an enemy, every player gains 1 Charge.
Whenever a player Exhaust a card, draw 1.
After the first reshuffle each combat draw +1 card.
The first exhaust card each combat returns to hand next turn.8+ Increase next keyword's value by +N.
Gain N Guard every turn while below half Vitals.4+
Strike X.The first card each player uses every combat gains +1 to its keyword value.
discard ALL cards at turn end.6+
Immediately reshuffle the discard pile into the draw pile.4+
Draw 2. Discard 1.Enemies never flee.

bs-(between-stabbings)/doctrines/heroic-.title: HEROIC-DOCTRINES
bs-(between-stabbings)/doctrines/heroic-.description:
10+ Ignore a reduction to 0 Vitals.

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/fleeing.title: FLEEING
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/fleeing.description:
If you're feeling outnumbered, outmatched, or both, your Hearth may always attempt to Flee a combat encounter. To do so, simply roll 2d6, subtract the number of remaining enemies, and compare the resulting number to the amount of players.Say there's 2x Husk.canvas, and 3x Shrieker.canvas pinning you down, your VITALS are going into single-digit territory, and your comrades decide you should all Flee. You roll 2d6. The result is a perfectly balanced 6 and a 1, giving a total of 7. There are 5 enemies total, so your roll of 7 becomes a 2. Since there are 4 of you, the attempt is failed, and you must progress with your turn as normal. You may re-attempt to Flee after either:
Your VITALS amount has changed (probably for the worse). OR Enemy numbers have changed (be it from murder or reinforcements).

cards/backbone_decay.html.title: BACKBONE_DECAY
cards/backbone_decay.html.description: Backbones lose 1 point of their bonus per Rest or Level-Up. Oaths of permanence: at character creation, the player chooses four cards that are locked in as their signature core, these Backbone cards will never Decay through rests, but can also never be removed or altered via SHARDS. ARTEFACTS and CURIOS still apply. That gives the Players: a stable identity, a way to keep some Backbone value permanently, Any other Backbone cards can still decay, evolve, or be replaced over time.

bs-(between-stabbings)/hq.html.title: HQ
bs-(between-stabbings)/hq.html.description:
Long-distance jobs improve the HQ through a staying-behind NPC. The HQ is a place that evolves because the party neglects it, which is a bit of a reversal: being away has an opportunity cost, but also generates progress through caretaking, repairs, or investment of locals the Hearth may have previously helped/hired. Use travel time as the main variable, named when giving out the assignment.  Example: Near, Local, Far, Remote, and Expedition; each band costs different amounts of in-world time to travel to and return (ex. 1h / 3h / 6h / 12h / 24h+, respectively).
Danger is tied to travel time. Near HQ, the return is easy; farther away, the route becomes slower, more dangerous, or more event-heavy. HQ effects scale by time band. Longer absences should produce stronger HQ gains, better caretaker progress, or larger downtime upgrades. Long travel creates risk or upkeep cost. Time spent away can also create pressure: supplies consumed, HQ neglected, opportunities lost, or complications on return.
Each 8-hour segment gives the Caretaker one hidden d6 roll. Keep the mechanic behind the screen, only revealing the results once the players arrive back at base. Matching dice results create Upgrade Pips. Any pair of identical results in the same batch counts as one point toward HQ facility progress.
The improvement that triggers is chosen by the matching value. 1-2 Gives a pip for the Quarters. 3-4 Gives a pip for the Workshop, and 5-6 gives a pip to the Archive.
The more dice rolled, the more likely a match becomes, but the rarer perfect uniform rolls stay. That creates rising odds without making the system predictable. Facility progress only happens at thresholds. Upgrade Points do not directly become effects; they unlock discrete facility upgrades once a required total is reached. Ruined - 5 pips -> Basic
Basic - 4 pips -> Improved
Improved - 5 pips -> Advanced
Advanced - 6 pips -> State of the Art For the tiering:
Ruined: not built yet.
Basic: simple, modest effect.
Improved: the signature effect that makes the facility distinct
Advanced: Restoring Backbone Decay between expeditions for the facility's chosen Prism.
State of the Art: +1 to a given Prism's Signature Backbone capacity
Facilities can start prebuilt! Some HQ areas may begin Ruined, Basic, or even Improved depending on fiction, so the GM should allow a ready-made medbay, arsenal, or archive when the story calls for it.
If you want some HQs to begin partially built, then “Ruined -> Basic” can just be the first step for places that start as ruins or blank shells, while established bases begin at Basic or Improved depending on lore. Basic: remove leftover statuses from the last expedition.
Improved: restore a single point of Prism loss after a Trauma hit.
Advanced: restore decayed Backbone bonus on Guile cards for the next expedition.
State of the Art: protect one Guile (per Ember) card from Backbone decay permanently. Basic: gain a random Tool before each expedition (+1 Per Tier).
Improved: Gain 1 Shard before each expedition (+1 at max Tier).
Advanced: restore decayed Backbone bonus on Might cards for the next expedition.
State of the Art: protect one Might (per Ember) card from Backbone decay permanently. Basic: Access to encountered enemy Loops and Doctrine Library
Improved: carry one Doctrine from your last expedition onto the next one (Max Tier: does not count against the standard amount of Doctrines per expedition).
Advanced: restore decayed Backbone bonus on Insight cards for the next expedition.

ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/compani.title: COMPANIONS
ds-(during-stabbings)/playerside/compani.description:
During EXPLORATION, you may encounter lost NPC's, or have to rescue one from a troublesome situation. This is where the COMPANIONS system comes in!
Each Companion has a set amount of Resolve points. These represent their engagement and desire to stick around. Resolve usually varies between 1-6 points, depending on the NPC and your relations with them. Companions also have their own unique Cards they add to the shared DECK during combats. Rescue targets will usually have low Resolve, causing them to flee early. Companions take their cards with them, once Resolve runs out. You will then have to seek them out again after the combat encounter is over.
Resolve is lost whenever the Hearth loses VITALS >= 25% of their max in a single turn. It cannot be gained back until the expedition is over.

landing-page.html.title: LANDING PAGE
landing-page.html.description:
By clicking or typing in the link to this site you have agreed to not share any content, ideas, or concepts you find here without direct permission from our developers *Ember Deck is a project still in early development, so this site may update and look unrecognisable after the fact!Said plainly, Ember Deck is a Dieselpunk TTRPG Deckbuilder made for (ideally) 3-4 Player groups, but it should still scale relatively well until about 5-6 people. It was an idea heavily inspired by gems such as "Slay The Spire", "Hearthstone", "Slice and Dice", and "Morimens". The gameplay features tight resource management in a low-math, high-keyword environment, giving a couple of anchor points to make Your own character and their deck unique and powerful, whereas the Setting provides plenty of opportunity for "angsty" or "against all odds" roleplay in a grimdark world, where magic is ubiquitous, but hope is scant.Once, there was HELL. Every day, souls passed in and out, getting processed, stripped bare, and sent to the Hall of Echoes, pure. One day an apocalyptic event happened. Because of that, HELL is working overtime and is still decades behind. Because of that, Souls get clogged up in the in-between, and the radiation that results from this pileup seeps into the living, and covers the world under a blanket of mist. Until finally, their bodies or minds can't take it anymore, and burn up, or hollow out, from the inside.The LORE dropdown is a good place to start for anyone curious about the world and the gritty reality found therein, ex.: VIBE, LORE>IN-WORLD, HEARTHS AND EMBERS. For the Rules Lawyers: here's the short end of it, as the detailed rulings fall under their own dropdowns, labelled BS, DS, CARDS, or CHARACTER.Once a concept of who you'd like to play as forms in your mind, the preferable place to go is the CHARACTER>OATHS Section, where you'll find all three major Oaths (which function like classes, and determine your starting Attributes - Prisms), these in turn determine your Starting Deck.
All Oaths start with 10 BACKBONE cards making up their deck, but the keywords may vary based on chosen Oath and even subtle differences between the same Oath's decks are possible at times.
Now, having a concept, embodied by an Oath, and a Deck, you select your CHARGE, and your Passives (Each Oath offers a different selection). These are the anchor points mentioned before - they'll define your playstyle heavily. Once that's in order, and your friends have done the same, you're set! (Don't forget to send at least 4 sentences worth of a backstory to your GM, though.)As with most RPGs, the weight of this point lands mostly on the GM, however some things to keep in mind as a Player skipping through sections: Checks are made with d6's, many of them at times (up to 8-ish). Miniatures are welcome but leave the rulers at home - combat doesn't use movement. Agree on a vision you and the other players have on your bond, how you met and connected, it will save the GM a couple of grey hairs. Enjoy! Changed Keyword function and Naming:
EMPOWER -> MOMENTUM.
EMPOWER was Changed to provide a readily accessible pool instead of a small trickle.
BRACE was updated to mirror this change.
Focus -> EMPOWER.
Ward now scales with Insight and prevents enemy keywords as well as statuses.
Delay was Removed.
Added CHARACTER/IMAGES sections across the vault to provide visuals.
CASE STUDY was removed for cleanup Certain notes have received related images inside the notes
CINDERS, and, HEARTHS AND EMBERS were moved to the FRACTIONS dropdown
The SYNC-AXIS is now more firmly tied to combat through the updated EMPOWERand BRACE keywords
CHARGE note was corrected, showing an improved formula for post-burst draw calculation
INVENTORY section was brushed up, adding needed specifications and removing unnecessary repetitions. Allowed Curios to have limited Mechanical value.
Clarified PIERCE's scaling
Removed false keyword scalings from GUILE, INSIGHT, and MIGHT
Clarified SHARDS in-combat effects, and removed Delay from the list.
Added the EXPLORATION section
Added Several Notes explaining the rules of EXPLORATION (NODE - RESTNODE - PATHNODE - UNKNOWNNODE - UNSTABLENODE - ENCOUNTER, and REFERENCE IMAGE)
Expanded the TURNS page with an infographic, which can also be found in ENEMIES>RULINGS
Added HEARTH SHEET as well as EMBER SHEET
Compiled existing notes into a single file INFECTION
Streamlined LIGHTNING
Made the HOLLOW effect more inconvenient Changed the Oath of the Crucible to Oath of the Pyre for easier differentiation and recognition from Conduit
Introduced Party-influenced STATUS cards added when taking DMG at 0 VITALS and reshuffling the shared deck during prolonged combats
Added the DECK note for clarifications of card handling
Expanded on the Tools and Curios section within INVENTORY
Altered MISTWALKER PASSIVES to DISCARDthe first status instead of resisting an enemy intent for wider versatility
Created Intent Loops for Firebomber.canvas and Cultist.canvas and deleted Cinder Goliath.png
Also added a short guide to reading Intent loops in the RULINGS section
Also in the RULINGS section, introduced a potentially volatile mechanic of Front and Back row enemies. To be tested.
Clarified interactions between MOMENTUM and AREA and LIGHTNING
Redefined OATH_OF_THE_PYRE CHARACTER/OATHS/PYRE/CHARGES to not rely on MOMENTUM so heavily, changing one entirely to propose a new playstyle
Added a third option for handling hitting 0 Vitals.
Changed SHARDS in-combat complexity Added several new Enemies, and expanded upon the existing ones
Changed Reshuffle rules: The Shared DECK now keeps the reshuffle count between combats and resets at rest sites only.

headers.heading: Welcome to the official Ember Deck Vault!

cards/status_cards/psychosis.html.title: PSYCHOSIS
cards/status_cards/psychosis.html.description: UNPLAYABLE. EXHAUST itself at turn end. When drawn, discard 1 card from your hand, OR lose 1 ENERGY. UNPLAYABLE. EXHAUST at turn end.

character/items/currency/currency.html.title: CURRENCY
character/items/currency/currency.html.description: Different cities approach the matter in their own ways, however in VOSS, the standard values are ROCKS, and their adjacents; PEBBLES, DUST, BOULDER, GEM. What makes them unique is their production process - city officials use specialised grids of water-jets to cut piercing, thin holes all the way through the coins, making them instantly recognisable just by brushing a finger against them in your pocket! Furthermore, this design prevents wear and tear, counterfeiting becomes a matter of owning a water-jet yourself, and there's added utility in the form of line-purses. Those being tough lines weaved through the holes to make bundles of coins, either for saving, preparing an exact sum, planning expenses, and more.

character/items/enhancements.html.title: ENHANCEMENTS
character/items/enhancements.html.description:
Enhancements are installed body augments. You may install any number. Each ENHANCEMENTS has an Enhancement Strain tied to it. Your body can support Enhancement Strain up to the SUM of your PRISMs (max of 18).
At TURN start, if your total Enhancement Strain is above your PRISM SUM, shuffle 1 PSYCHOSIS into your discard pile for each point over the limit.
PSYCHOSIS: When drawn, discard 1 card from your hand, OR lose 1 ENERGY. UNPLAYABLE. EXHAUST at turn end.
This check is made per Ember, so multiple party members can each generate their own PSYCHOSIS. Strain: 7. At the start of combat, add 1 STRIKE 15 EXHAUST card to your hand. In Exploration, this counts as a ranged weapon system for relevant BS ROLL CHECKS. Lungs Strain:5. Filter Mist, gas, or smoke effects rendering them harmless to You only. (Enhancement counterpart to the Masks/Filters in Tools)

Strain: 10. Restore 1 Vitals at Turn Start.
Strain: 4/8. T1: Gain Advantage on one/any physical BS ROLL CHECK category tied to that limb, such as climbing, lifting, balance, breaking, or forcing. They may also substitute for a Tool or Gatekeep when the fiction clearly supports it. T2: Gain +1 STRIKE DMG.
Strain: 6. Gain Advantage on one physical BS ROLL CHECK category tied to that limb, such as climbing, lifting, balance, breaking, or forcing. They may also substitute for a Tool or Gatekeep when the fiction clearly supports it.
Strain: 6 Filter Mist, gas, or smoke effects, rendering them harmless to you only. This is the implant counterpart to the Mask / Face Wrap Tool effect.
Strain: 4/6. T1: Increase Tool carrying capacity by 1. T2: Increase Tool carrying capacity by 2.
Strain: 2/4/6 (you can install in tiers of coverage). T1: Every 7 hits, take 1 DMG less. T2: Every 5th hit, take 1 DMG less. T3: Every 3rd hit, take 1 DMG less.Tip: Two T1 Enhancements work together as a T2 in the case of party-wide stacking calculation
Strain: 6/9. T1: Gain Advantage on sight-based BS ROLL CHECKs. T2: Night vision.
Strain: 12. Increase all installed enhancements' effects by +1 (Works with: MLS, Heart, Arms, Legs, Spine, Optics)

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: Amber Ribbon Casino
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: The Amber Ribbon embraces affordable elegance without coming off as too Upper-classy. Warm amber lighting fills the gaming halls, creating an atmosphere designed to make patrons feel wealthier than they actually are. Professional musicians perform nightly while servers glide between tables carrying drinks. The casino's management understands that most customers come seeking excitement rather than profit. Wealthy merchants mingle with ambitious workers hoping for a lucky evening. Security remains discreet but highly effective. The Amber Ribbon has perfected the art of making people enjoy losing money over the course of a dozen games.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/Amber Ribbon Casino.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: Boulderkeep Detention Center
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: Boulderkeep occupies an unusual space between prison and financial institution. The facility earned its nickname from the common practice of paying a BOULDER to secure someone's release. Wealthy offenders often treat confinement here as a temporary inconvenience. The accommodations are comfortable enough to inspire public outrage on a regular basis. Many inmates leave Boulderkeep with lighter wallets but otherwise pleasantly unchanged lives.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/Boulderkeep Detention Center.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: Clothes Contact Mall
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: Clothes Contact Mall serves as the city's fast fashion capital. The enormous complex contains everything from luxury tailoring boutiques to industrial uniform suppliers. Wealthy shoppers browse oddities alongside factory workers purchasing practical clothing. Tailors, uniform suppliers, luxury boutiques, and industrial outfitters compete under one roof. If you cannot find clothing here, it probably does not exist anywhere else.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/Clothes Contact Mall.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: Dead Walk
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: Nobody remembers what Dead Walk was called before it earned its current name. Prison transfers, military funerals, and disaster memorials have all passed through these streets for generations now. The buildings seem perpetually tilted away from the outskirts of the city, as if shying away from the path this road leads down. Locals swear conversations become quieter when walking here.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/Dead Walk.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: Emerald Street
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: Emerald Street represents wealth concentrated into a single avenue. Jewelers, luxury boutiques, private clinics, financial advisors, and prestigious service providers compete for elite clientele. The street's architecture is immaculate, with polished stone facades and decorative metalwork visible everywhere. Security patrols maintain a constant but unobtrusive presence. Residents of poorer districts often view Emerald Street as proof that they inhabit a different city entirely.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/Emerald Street.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: Fuct Avenue
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: Even city planners have stopped pretending the name is temporary. Every failed business, broken promise, and desperate scheme eventually seems to end up here. Buildings lean at questionable angles, infrastructure barely functions, and regulations seem entirely theoretical. If someone says they live on Fuct Avenue, nobody asks follow-up questions.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/Fuct Avenue.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: Last Chance High Security Prison
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description:
Last Chance dominates the skyline like a fortress of black concrete and riveted steel. Layers of thick walls, electrified fencing, watchtowers, and anti-vehicle barriers surround the facility, giving the impression that the prison expects an army trying to break someone out from the outside, rather than escape attempts on the inside.
The city's most dangerous criminals, political enemies, gang leaders, and inconvenient bystanders all eventually find their way here. Searchlights sweep the surrounding streets day and night. What everyone agrees on is simple: if someone is sent to Last Chance, most people consider them gone.

headers.heading: a. Last Chance High Security Prison

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/Last Chance High Security Prison.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: Lonely Alley
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: Lonely Alley is narrow enough that neighbouring buildings nearly touch overhead. The passage receives little attention from city planners. Despite its name, people constantly arrange secret meetings here.Informants, lovers, criminals, and politicians all make use of its privacy. For a place called Lonely Alley, it witnesses a surprising amount of company.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/Lonely Alley.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: Redemption Low Security Prison
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: Redemption was built on the optimistic belief that people could change. Compared to most facilities of its kind, it resembles a strange combination of prison, factory, and boarding school. Inmates spend much of their time working trades, maintaining gardens, or learning practical skills. The prison's workshops produce furniture, textiles, and machinery parts sold throughout the city. Critics argue the facility is simply a source of cheap labour wrapped in optimistic language. Supporters point to former inmates who have successfully rebuilt their lives. Families often visit on weekends, creating an atmosphere almost unheard of elsewhere. Residents living nearby joke that Redemption contains fewer criminals than the city district surrounding it.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/Redemption Low Security Prison.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: Teal Highway
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: The Teal Highway is old Main way into Voss. Originally painted with bright teal lane markings decades ago, the colour has long since faded, but the name remained. Massive freight trucks, military convoys, fuel tankers, and armored transports used to thunder across its lanes around the clock. Entire neighborhoods owe their existence to businesses that served highway traffic. Diners, repair garages, warehouses, and fuel depots stretch for kilometers alongside it, now in a sorry state without much going on. Crossing it on foot used to be considered a minor act of bravery.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/Teal Highway.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Awkward Guard Café
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: The Awkward Guard Café sits directly across from Last Chance Prison, guards spend breaks there discussing inmates, paperwork, and supervisors while pretending not to discuss any of those things. The atmosphere becomes noticeably tense whenever unfamiliar faces enter. Strong coffee has made the café something of an institution. The staff have become experts at avoiding sensitive conversations, and most tables offer clear views of the prison gates. Most customers sit facing the prison windows out of habit.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Awkward Guard Café.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The BIG Feather
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: The largest newspaper in the city occupies an entire block of printing presses and filing cabinets. Its archives reportedly contain records on everyone who has mattered and many who have not. Politicians fear it, bribe it, and citizens read it religiously. Critics accuse it of arrogance, sensationalism, and reporters who pursue stories with a persistence bordering on obsession. Supporters argue those qualities are necessary for effective journalism. Either way, nearly everyone reads it. The publication has exposed corruption scandals, criminal networks, and corporate conspiracies for decades. Not that anything further's been done about them after the fact, though.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The BIG Feather.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Cinder Harbor
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: Cinder Harbor is the oldest and most feared section of the city. Rusting cranes loom over abandoned warehouses while thick fog rolls in from the river and surrounding wastes almost every hour of every day. Missing persons reports originating near the harbor outnumber those from the rest of the city. Authorities patrol the area regularly but rarely stay longer than comfortable, which doesn't offer a lot of coverage. Even hardened criminals prefer conducting business elsewhere whenever possible. Rumors speak of Cinders operating among the warehouses. The stories become considerably darker once that information enters the conversation.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Cinder Harbor.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Crafty Carpet Store
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: The Crafty Carpet Store presents itself as a humble family-owned business. The carpets are excellent, the prices reasonable, and the customer service suspiciously efficient., which complicates investigations considerably. Customs officials, police inspectors, and tax auditors have spent years attempting to uncover evidence of wrongdoing. None have succeeded. Employees remain unfailingly polite regardless of questioning. Locals openly refer to the business as a smuggling front while shopping there. The owners neither confirm nor deny anything and continue selling quality carpets.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Crafty Carpet Store.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Crystal Quill Casino
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: Where the Amber Ribbon focuses on glamour, the Crystal Quill focuses on sophistication. Its architecture resembles a luxurious library crossed with a palace ballroom. Writers, journalists, academics, lawyers, and civil servants form much of its clientele. The casino hosts literary events, debates, and lectures alongside traditional gaming. Wealthy individuals appreciate the illusion that their betting habits are somehow more refined, while Wealthy intellectuals appreciate not being forced to participate. Crystal chandeliers illuminate every room. Many fortunes have been lost as signed checks, in perfect handwriting.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Crystal Quill Casino.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Golden Boar Drugstore
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: The Golden Boar is one of the oldest pharmacies in the city, recognizable by the polished brass boar mounted above its entrance. Officially, it sells medicines, tonics, herbal remedies, stimulants, and household necessities. Unofficially, it sells a great many things that never appear on invoices. The owner has cultivated a reputation for helping customers acquire exactly what they need without asking unnecessary questions. The owner has survived six police raids and emerged wealthier every time. Somehow, the Golden Boar always seems fully stocked the following morning.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Golden Boar Drugstore.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Great Hearth Church
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: The largest chapterhouse of the Great Hearth movement dominates an entire district. According to their doctrine, loneliness, conflict, and suffering stem from separation between individuals. Critics describe the movement as a cult obsessed with erasing personal identity, The Church's leadership responds by pointing to their efficient administration, low crime rates, and extensive charitable programs. Dissidents disappear quickly enough to keep peace. Streets under the Church's management are cleaner and safer than most parts of the city... The only catch is the expectation of ideological loyalty. Many citizens dislike the Church intensely while secretly wishing their own districts were managed half as well.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Great Hearth Church.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Happy Gun
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: The Happy Gun somehow manages to be both the friendliest and most alarming gun shop in the city. Its bright signage and cheerful branding contrast sharply with the rows of weapons displayed inside. The owner greets customers with alarming enthusiasm and impeccable product knowledge. Bounty hunters, security officers, criminals, and collectors all pass through its doors. The smiling revolver on the sign has become a local landmark.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Happy Gun.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Humble Carrot Deli.co
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: The Humble Carrot began as a small delivery company and expanded into a logistical empire. Its orange-clad couriers can be found in every district at nearly every hour. They transport documents, food, medicine, machinery parts, and dart through traffic with reckless efficiency. The company's efficiency borders on supernatural. The city's economy depends on the Humble Carrot far more than most people realize.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Humble Carrot Deli.co.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Hungry Dog Bistro
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: The food is cheap, filling, and the subject of constant rumours. Jokes about dog food have circulated for years, yet the dining room remains packed every evening. Portions are enormous, prices are low, and service is remarkably fast. Workers finishing long shifts often choose practicality over culinary excellence.. Whatever the ingredients are, they keep people coming back.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Hungry Dog Bistro.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Onyx
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: Nobody agrees on what the Onyx actually is. Some call it a nightclub, others a private club, and a few insist it is an exterior reflects almost no light, giving the building an unsettling presence regardless of the time of day. Whatever happens inside tends to stay inside.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Onyx.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Sneaky Hazelnut PI
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: A private investigation office operating from a surprisingly cozy storefront. Its owner claims the hazelnut represents hidden truths buried beneath hard shells. Missing persons, cheating spouses, corporate espionage; nothing is too small or too strange. If the Sneaky Hazelnut cannot find the answer, it can usually find someone who owes it one.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Sneaky Hazelnut PI.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Talon Bar
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: The Talon attracts mercenaries, bounty hunters, and people who want others to think they are mercenaries and bounty hunters, all gather beneath its steel-beamed ceiling. The walls display weapons, trophies, and famous last words of those who acquired the two. Fights occur regularly but are surprisingly organized. Fights are common, though surprisingly professional. The bartender keeps score. Newcomers often mistake the establishment for a dangerous place. In reality, it is usually safer than many supposedly respectable venues elsewhere due to the sheer amount of armed people who want nothing more than take a break.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Talon Bar.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Trusty Grape
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: A respectable winehouse where merchants close deals and politicians and gang members alike pretend not to be either. Its cellar is larger than the building itself, leading to endless speculation about what else might be stored down there. The wine selection is extensive enough to impress genuine connoisseurs. If you need information, someone at the Trusty Grape probably already has it, and a price in mind.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Trusty Grape.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Weeping Gull
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: Standing beside the riverfront docks, the Weeping Gull has endured floods, strikes, riots, and more than one naval bombardment. The tavern's interior smells permanently of river water, engine grease, tobacco smoke, and old wood. Every table bears carved initials from decades of visitors. Dockworkers begin their mornings here while sailors end their nights in the same seats. If something important happens beyond the city's borders, odds are someone here already knows about it.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Weeping Gull.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: The Wet Goose
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description: The Wet Goose sits as far from the river as geography allows, in one of the driest parts of the city. The origin of its name changes depending on which regular you ask and how much they've had to drink, but all of them involve alcohol. The tavern's walls are decorated with paintings, sculptures, and carvings of increasingly miserable geese. The owner encourages customers to donate new goose-related artwork. The beer is cheap, and the arguments are free. It attracts workers, students, off-duty guards, and anyone looking for an uncomplicated evening. Most people come for the beer but return for the exchanges between long-time patrons.
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.sourcePath: LORE/WORLD/PHYSICAL PILLAR/LOCATIONS/CITY OF VOSS/The Wet Goose.md

lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.title: VOSS
lore/world/physical-pillar/locations/cit.description:
Cobbler's Road sprawls beyond the northern walls, where city authority becomes more of a suggestion than a reality. Cheap housing, struggling businesses, workshops, and temporary shelters dominate the district. It attracts laborers, debtors, drifters, and anyone who cannot afford life inside the walls. Fuct Avenue forms its miserable heart, serving as a monument to bad decisions and worse luck. Lonely Alley cuts through the district's interior, providing privacy for meetings nobody wants witnessed. Cobbler's Road is poor, overcrowded, and frequently overlooked.
o. Fuct Avenue
u. Lonely Alley
z. The Sneaky Hazelnut PI Lowgate hugs the northern wall, living permanently beneath the shadow of Last Chance High Security Prison. Guards, prison workers, and families of inmates make up much of the district's North-side population. The Awkward Guard Café serves as an unofficial gathering place for correctional staff, while the Wet Goose provides a less respectable alternative. The Trusty Grape handles much of the district's business dealings, both legal and otherwise. Security presence is heavy, though residents have long since stopped noticing armed patrols. Visitors often find Lowgate oppressive, but locals consider it simply predictable and strict.
a. Last Chance High Security Prison
e. The Wet Goose
g. The Trusty Grape
j. The Awkward Guard Café Black Topper serves as the city's commercial workhorse, the name being a reference to the black caps used for cans of diesel. Warehouses, wholesalers, delivery depots, retail shops, and service businesses crowd its streets from dawn until well after dark. The Golden Boar Drugstore supplies half the city with medicine, while the Humble Carrot's delivery network keeps goods moving between districts. Clothes Contact Mall draws merchants, workers, and suppliers from nearly every corner of the city. Traffic is constant, if something needs to be bought, shipped, repaired, or acquired on short notice, Black Topper is usually the answer. Few residents describe the district as beautiful, but almost everyone depends on its services.
f. The Golden Boar Drugstore
v. The Humble Carrot Deli.co
x. Clothes Contact Mall Clay-Knot sits near the geographical and political center of the city. Emerald Street showcases the city's most prestigious businesses, while Boulderkeep Detention Center quietly demonstrates how differently justice can function for different people, and the Redemption Low-Sec Prison provides a more optimistic face to incarceration than most citizens are accustomed to. The Great Hearth Church maintains a powerful presence throughout the district, funding services, taking care of neighborhoods, and recruiting new followers daily. The Crystal Quill Casino attracts professionals, officials, and ambitious social climbers. More important decisions are made in Clay-Knot than anywhere else in the city.
b. Redemption Low Security Prison
l. The Great Hearth Church
p. Amber Ribbon Casino
q. The Crystal Quill Casino
s. Boulderkeep Detention Center
t. Emerald Street Greenhill is where influence prefers to appear respectable. The BIG Feather dominates the local skyline and reputation alike, with its reporters constantly digging into matters somebody would rather keep buried. The Happy Gun serves hunters, security personnel, and private citizens alike, though opinions differ on whether its cheerful branding is amusing or disturbing. Compared to neighbouring districts, Greenhill feels orderly and well-maintained, with the majority of the middle class being housed here. Political conversations are common here, despite a lack of politicians.
c. Teal Highway
i. The Happy Gun
w. The BIG Feather Watcher's Walk lies beyond the southern walls along the riverbanks, where the city gradually gives way to water, industry, and the Mist. Docks, warehouses, boarding houses, and riverside businesses dominate the landscape. The Weeping Gull serves as a gathering place for sailors, dockworkers, and anyone seeking information arriving from beyond the city. Dead Walk passes through the district, adding an atmosphere many residents have simply learned to live with. Further downstream sits Cinder Harbor, a place whose reputation grows darker with every retelling. Along the bridges and riverbanks lies the subdistrict of Bounty Bridge, where bounty hunters, debt collectors, and other professionals of flexible morality frequently conduct business. Watcher's Walk rarely sleeps. The river ensures that something is always moving, and the Mist tends to keep people awake.
d. Dead Walk
h. The Weeping Gull
n. The Cinder Harbor Wolfkey occupies the south-eastern portion of the city and has earned a reputation as a place where people go when they do not wish to be found. Narrow alleys, interconnected courtyards, hidden entrances, and aging tenements create a maze-like environment that confuses outsiders. The Crafty Carpet Store conducts its suspiciously successful business here, while the Talon Bar attracts mercenaries and bounty hunters from across the region. The Onyx remains Wolfkey's greatest mystery, drawing influential visitors without ever explaining why. The district contains countless safehouses, backroom operations, and businesses that avoid being thought about too long. Criminals, informants, investigators, and legitimate residents coexist in uneasy balance. Wolfkey is not lawless, but it sure as hell walks that line.
k. The Talon Bar
m. The Crafty Carpet Store
r. The Onyx
y. The Hungry Dog Bistro

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.
